2273static const char *set_enable_sendfile(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
2274                                   const char *arg)
2275{
2276    core_dir_config *d = d_;
2277
2278    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on") == 0) {
2279        d->enable_sendfile = ENABLE_SENDFILE_ON;
2280    }
2281    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off") == 0) {
2282        d->enable_sendfile = ENABLE_SENDFILE_OFF;
2283    }
2284    else {
2285        return "parameter must be 'on' or 'off'";
2286    }
2287
2288    return NULL;
2289}
2290
2291static const char *set_read_buf_size(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
2292                                     const char *arg)
2293{
2294    core_dir_config *d = d_;
2295    apr_off_t size;
2296    char *end;
2297
2298    if (apr_strtoff(&size, arg, &end, 10)
2299            || *end || size < 0 || size > APR_UINT32_MAX)
2300        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool,
2301                           "parameter must be a number between 0 and "
2302                           APR_STRINGIFY(APR_UINT32_MAX) "): ",
2303                           arg, NULL);
2304
2305    d->read_buf_size = (apr_size_t)size;
2306
2307    return NULL;
2308}
2309
2310static const char *set_flush_max_threshold(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
2311                                           const char *arg)
2312{
2313    core_server_config *conf =
2314        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
2315    apr_off_t size;
2316    char *end;
2317
2318    if (apr_strtoff(&size, arg, &end, 10)
2319            || *end || size < 0 || size > APR_UINT32_MAX)
2320        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool,
2321                           "parameter must be a number between 0 and "
2322                           APR_STRINGIFY(APR_UINT32_MAX) "): ",
2323                           arg, NULL);
2324
2325    conf->flush_max_threshold = (apr_size_t)size;
2326
2327    return NULL;
2328}
2329
2330static const char *set_flush_max_pipelined(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
2331                                           const char *arg)
2332{
2333    core_server_config *conf =
2334        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
2335    apr_off_t num;
2336    char *end;
2337
2338    if (apr_strtoff(&num, arg, &end, 10)
2339            || *end || num < -1 || num > APR_INT32_MAX)
2340        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool,
2341                           "parameter must be a number between -1 and "
2342                           APR_STRINGIFY(APR_INT32_MAX) ": ",
2343                           arg, NULL);
2344
2345    conf->flush_max_pipelined = (apr_int32_t)num;
2346
2347    return NULL;
2348}
2349
2350/*
2351 * Report a missing-'>' syntax error.
2352 */ view japanese
2353static char *unclosed_directive(cmd_parms *cmd)
2354{
2355    return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, cmd->cmd->name,
2356                       "> directive missing closing '>'", NULL);
2357}
2358
2359/*
2360 * Report a missing args in '<Foo >' syntax error.
2361 */ view japanese
2362static char *missing_container_arg(cmd_parms *cmd)
2363{
2364    return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, cmd->cmd->name,
2365                       "> directive requires additional arguments", NULL);
2366}
2367
2368AP_CORE_DECLARE_NONSTD(const char *) ap_limit_section(cmd_parms *cmd,
2369                                                      void *dummy,
2370                                                      const char *arg)
2371{
2372    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2373    const char *limited_methods;
2374    void *tog = cmd->cmd->cmd_data;
2375    apr_int64_t limited = 0;
2376    apr_int64_t old_limited = cmd->limited;
2377    const char *errmsg;
2378
2379    if (endp == NULL) {
2380        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2381    }
2382
2383    limited_methods = apr_pstrmemdup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2384
2385    if (!limited_methods[0]) {
2386        return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2387    }
2388
2389    while (limited_methods[0]) {
2390        char *method = ap_getword_conf(cmd->temp_pool, &limited_methods);
2391        int methnum;
2392
2393        /* check for builtin or module registered method number */ view japanese
2394        methnum = ap_method_number_of(method);
2395
2396        if (methnum == M_TRACE && !tog) {
2397            return "TRACE cannot be controlled by <Limit>, see TraceEnable";
2398        }
2399        else if (methnum == M_INVALID) {
2400            /* method has not been registered yet, but resource restriction
2401             * is always checked before method handling, so register it.
2402             */ view japanese
2403            if (cmd->pool == cmd->temp_pool) {
2404                /* In .htaccess, we can't globally register new methods. */ view japanese
2405                return apr_psprintf(cmd->pool, "Could not register method '%s' "
2406                                   "for %s from .htaccess configuration",
2407                                    method, cmd->cmd->name);
2408            }
2409            methnum = ap_method_register(cmd->pool,
2410                                         apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, method));
2411        }
2412
2413        limited |= (AP_METHOD_BIT << methnum);
2414    }
2415
2416    /* Killing two features with one function,
2417     * if (tog == NULL) <Limit>, else <LimitExcept>
2418     */ view japanese
2419    limited = tog ? ~limited : limited;
2420
2421    if (!(old_limited & limited)) {
2422        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, cmd->cmd->name,
2423                           "> directive excludes all methods", NULL);
2424    }
2425    else if ((old_limited & limited) == old_limited) {
2426        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, cmd->cmd->name,
2427                           "> directive specifies methods already excluded",
2428                           NULL);
2429    }
2430
2431    cmd->limited &= limited;
2432
2433    errmsg = ap_walk_config(cmd->directive->first_child, cmd, cmd->context);
2434
2435    cmd->limited = old_limited;
2436
2437    return errmsg;
2438}
2439
2440/* XXX: Bogus - need to do this differently (at least OS2/Netware suffer
2441 * the same problem!!!
2442 * We use this in <DirectoryMatch> and <FilesMatch>, to ensure that
2443 * people don't get bitten by wrong-cased regex matches
2444 */ view japanese
2445
2446#ifdef WIN32
2447#define USE_ICASE AP_REG_ICASE
2448#else
2449#define USE_ICASE 0
2450#endif
2451
2452static const char *dirsection(cmd_parms *cmd, void *mconfig, const char *arg)
2453{
2454    const char *errmsg;
2455    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2456    int old_overrides = cmd->override;
2457    char *old_path = cmd->path;
2458    core_dir_config *conf;
2459    ap_conf_vector_t *new_dir_conf = ap_create_per_dir_config(cmd->pool);
2460    ap_regex_t *r = NULL;
2461    const command_rec *thiscmd = cmd->cmd;
2462
2463    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
2464    if (err != NULL) {
2465        return err;
2466    }
2467
2468    if (endp == NULL) {
2469        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2470    }
2471
2472    arg = apr_pstrndup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2473
2474    if (!arg[0]) {
2475        return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2476    }
2477
2478    cmd->path = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2479    cmd->override = OR_ALL|ACCESS_CONF;
2480
2481    if (!strcmp(cmd->path, "~")) {
2482        cmd->path = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2483        if (!cmd->path)
2484            return "<Directory ~ > block must specify a path";
2485        r = ap_pregcomp(cmd->pool, cmd->path, AP_REG_EXTENDED|USE_ICASE);
2486        if (!r) {
2487            return "Regex could not be compiled";
2488        }
2489    }
2490    else if (thiscmd->cmd_data) { /* <DirectoryMatch> */ view japanese
2491        r = ap_pregcomp(cmd->pool, cmd->path, AP_REG_EXTENDED|USE_ICASE);
2492        if (!r) {
2493            return "Regex could not be compiled";
2494        }
2495    }
2496    else if (strcmp(cmd->path, "/") != 0)
2497    {
2498        char *newpath;
2499
2500        /*
2501         * Ensure that the pathname is canonical, and append the trailing /
2502         */ view japanese
2503        apr_status_t rv = apr_filepath_merge(&newpath, NULL, cmd->path,
2504                                             APR_FILEPATH_TRUENAME, cmd->pool);
2505        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && rv != APR_EPATHWILD) {
2506            return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "<Directory \"", cmd->path,
2507                               "\"> path is invalid.", NULL);
2508        }
2509
2510        cmd->path = newpath;
2511        if (cmd->path[strlen(cmd->path) - 1] != '/')
2512            cmd->path = apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, cmd->path, "/", NULL);
2513    }
2514
2515    /* initialize our config and fetch it */
2516    conf = ap_set_config_vectors(cmd->server, new_dir_conf, cmd->path,
2517                                 &core_module, cmd->pool);
2518
2519    errmsg = ap_walk_config(cmd->directive->first_child, cmd, new_dir_conf);
2520    if (errmsg != NULL)
2521        return errmsg;
2522
2523    conf->r = r;
2524    conf->d = cmd->path;
2525    conf->d_is_fnmatch = (apr_fnmatch_test(conf->d) != 0);
2526
2527    if (r) {
2528        conf->refs = apr_array_make(cmd->pool, 8, sizeof(char *));
2529        ap_regname(r, conf->refs, AP_REG_MATCH, 1);
2530    }
2531
2532    /* Make this explicit - the "/" root has 0 elements, that is, we
2533     * will always merge it, and it will always sort and merge first.
2534     * All others are sorted and tested by the number of slashes.
2535     */ view japanese
2536    if (strcmp(conf->d, "/") == 0)
2537        conf->d_components = 0;
2538    else
2539        conf->d_components = ap_count_dirs(conf->d);
2540
2541    ap_add_per_dir_conf(cmd->server, new_dir_conf);
2542
2543    if (*arg != '\0') {
2544        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "Multiple ", thiscmd->name,
2545                           "> arguments not (yet) supported.", NULL);
2546    }
2547
2548    cmd->path = old_path;
2549    cmd->override = old_overrides;
2550
2551    return NULL;
2552}
2553
2554static const char *urlsection(cmd_parms *cmd, void *mconfig, const char *arg)
2555{
2556    const char *errmsg;
2557    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2558    int old_overrides = cmd->override;
2559    char *old_path = cmd->path;
2560    core_dir_config *conf;
2561    ap_regex_t *r = NULL;
2562    const command_rec *thiscmd = cmd->cmd;
2563    ap_conf_vector_t *new_url_conf = ap_create_per_dir_config(cmd->pool);
2564    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
2565    if (err != NULL) {
2566        return err;
2567    }
2568
2569    if (endp == NULL) {
2570        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2571    }
2572
2573    arg = apr_pstrndup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2574
2575    if (!arg[0]) {
2576        return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2577    }
2578
2579    cmd->path = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2580    cmd->override = OR_ALL|ACCESS_CONF;
2581
2582    if (thiscmd->cmd_data) { /* <LocationMatch> */ view japanese
2583        r = ap_pregcomp(cmd->pool, cmd->path, AP_REG_EXTENDED);
2584        if (!r) {
2585            return "Regex could not be compiled";
2586        }
2587    }
2588    else if (!strcmp(cmd->path, "~")) {
2589        cmd->path = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2590        r = ap_pregcomp(cmd->pool, cmd->path, AP_REG_EXTENDED);
2591        if (!r) {
2592            return "Regex could not be compiled";
2593        }
2594    }
2595
2596    /* initialize our config and fetch it */
2597    conf = ap_set_config_vectors(cmd->server, new_url_conf, cmd->path,
2598                                 &core_module, cmd->pool);
2599
2600    errmsg = ap_walk_config(cmd->directive->first_child, cmd, new_url_conf);
2601    if (errmsg != NULL)
2602        return errmsg;
2603
2604    conf->d = apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, cmd->path);     /* No mangling, please */ view japanese
2605    conf->d_is_fnmatch = apr_fnmatch_test(conf->d) != 0;
2606    conf->r = r;
2607
2608    if (r) {
2609        conf->refs = apr_array_make(cmd->pool, 8, sizeof(char *));
2610        ap_regname(r, conf->refs, AP_REG_MATCH, 1);
2611    }
2612
2613    ap_add_per_url_conf(cmd->server, new_url_conf);
2614
2615    if (*arg != '\0') {
2616        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "Multiple ", thiscmd->name,
2617                           "> arguments not (yet) supported.", NULL);
2618    }
2619
2620    cmd->path = old_path;
2621    cmd->override = old_overrides;
2622
2623    return NULL;
2624}
2625
2626static const char *filesection(cmd_parms *cmd, void *mconfig, const char *arg)
2627{
2628    const char *errmsg;
2629    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2630    int old_overrides = cmd->override;
2631    char *old_path = cmd->path;
2632    core_dir_config *conf;
2633    ap_regex_t *r = NULL;
2634    const command_rec *thiscmd = cmd->cmd;
2635    ap_conf_vector_t *new_file_conf = ap_create_per_dir_config(cmd->pool);
2636    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd,
2637                                           NOT_IN_LOCATION | NOT_IN_LIMIT);
2638
2639    if (err != NULL) {
2640        return err;
2641    }
2642
2643    if (endp == NULL) {
2644        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2645    }
2646
2647    arg = apr_pstrndup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2648
2649    if (!arg[0]) {
2650        return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2651    }
2652
2653    cmd->path = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2654    /* Only if not an .htaccess file */
2655    if (!old_path) {
2656        cmd->override = OR_ALL|ACCESS_CONF;
2657    }
2658
2659    if (thiscmd->cmd_data) { /* <FilesMatch> */ view japanese
2660        r = ap_pregcomp(cmd->pool, cmd->path, AP_REG_EXTENDED|USE_ICASE);
2661        if (!r) {
2662            return "Regex could not be compiled";
2663        }
2664    }
2665    else if (!strcmp(cmd->path, "~")) {
2666        cmd->path = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2667        r = ap_pregcomp(cmd->pool, cmd->path, AP_REG_EXTENDED|USE_ICASE);
2668        if (!r) {
2669            return "Regex could not be compiled";
2670        }
2671    }
2672    else {
2673        char *newpath;
2674        /* Ensure that the pathname is canonical, but we
2675         * can't test the case/aliases without a fixed path */ view japanese
2676        if (apr_filepath_merge(&newpath, "", cmd->path,
2677                               0, cmd->pool) != APR_SUCCESS)
2678                return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "<Files \"", cmd->path,
2679                               "\"> is invalid.", NULL);
2680        cmd->path = newpath;
2681    }
2682
2683    /* initialize our config and fetch it */
2684    conf = ap_set_config_vectors(cmd->server, new_file_conf, cmd->path,
2685                                 &core_module, cmd->pool);
2686
2687    errmsg = ap_walk_config(cmd->directive->first_child, cmd, new_file_conf);
2688    if (errmsg != NULL)
2689        return errmsg;
2690
2691    conf->d = cmd->path;
2692    conf->d_is_fnmatch = apr_fnmatch_test(conf->d) != 0;
2693    conf->r = r;
2694
2695    if (r) {
2696        conf->refs = apr_array_make(cmd->pool, 8, sizeof(char *));
2697        ap_regname(r, conf->refs, AP_REG_MATCH, 1);
2698    }
2699
2700    ap_add_file_conf(cmd->pool, (core_dir_config *)mconfig, new_file_conf);
2701
2702    if (*arg != '\0') {
2703        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "Multiple ", thiscmd->name,
2704                           "> arguments not (yet) supported.", NULL);
2705    }
2706
2707    cmd->path = old_path;
2708    cmd->override = old_overrides;
2709
2710    return NULL;
2711}
2712
2713#define COND_IF      ((void *)1)
2714#define COND_ELSE    ((void *)2)
2715#define COND_ELSEIF  ((void *)3)
2716
2717static const char *ifsection(cmd_parms *cmd, void *mconfig, const char *arg)
2718{
2719    const char *errmsg;
2720    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2721    int old_overrides = cmd->override;
2722    char *old_path = cmd->path;
2723    core_dir_config *conf;
2724    const command_rec *thiscmd = cmd->cmd;
2725    ap_conf_vector_t *new_if_conf = ap_create_per_dir_config(cmd->pool);
2726    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_LIMIT);
2727    const char *condition;
2728    const char *expr_err;
2729
2730    if (err != NULL) {
2731        return err;
2732    }
2733
2734    if (endp == NULL) {
2735        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2736    }
2737
2738    arg = apr_pstrndup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2739
2740    /*
2741     * Set a dummy value so that other directives notice that they are inside
2742     * a config section.
2743     */ view japanese
2744    cmd->path = "*If";
2745    /* Only if not an .htaccess file */ view japanese
2746    if (!old_path) {
2747        cmd->override = OR_ALL|ACCESS_CONF;
2748    }
2749
2750    /* initialize our config and fetch it */ view japanese
2751    conf = ap_set_config_vectors(cmd->server, new_if_conf, cmd->path,
2752                                 &core_module, cmd->pool);
2753
2754    if (cmd->cmd->cmd_data == COND_IF)
2755        conf->condition_ifelse = AP_CONDITION_IF;
2756    else if (cmd->cmd->cmd_data == COND_ELSEIF)
2757        conf->condition_ifelse = AP_CONDITION_ELSEIF;
2758    else if (cmd->cmd->cmd_data == COND_ELSE)
2759        conf->condition_ifelse = AP_CONDITION_ELSE;
2760    else
2761        ap_assert(0);
2762
2763    if (conf->condition_ifelse == AP_CONDITION_ELSE) {
2764        if (arg[0])
2765            return "<Else> does not take an argument";
2766    }
2767    else {
2768        if (!arg[0])
2769            return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2770        condition = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
2771        conf->condition = ap_expr_parse_cmd(cmd, condition, 0, &expr_err, NULL);
2772        if (expr_err)
2773            return apr_psprintf(cmd->pool, "Cannot parse condition clause: %s",
2774                                expr_err);
2775    }
2776
2777    errmsg = ap_walk_config(cmd->directive->first_child, cmd, new_if_conf);
2778    if (errmsg != NULL)
2779        return errmsg;
2780
2781    conf->d = cmd->path;
2782    conf->d_is_fnmatch = 0;
2783    conf->r = NULL;
2784
2785    errmsg = ap_add_if_conf(cmd->pool, (core_dir_config *)mconfig, new_if_conf);
2786    if (errmsg != NULL)
2787        return errmsg;
2788
2789    if (*arg != '\0') {
2790        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "Multiple ", thiscmd->name,
2791                           "> arguments not supported.", NULL);
2792    }
2793
2794    cmd->path = old_path;
2795    cmd->override = old_overrides;
2796
2797    return NULL;
2798}
2799
2800static module *find_module(server_rec *s, const char *name)
2801{
2802    module *found = ap_find_linked_module(name);
2803
2804    /* search prelinked stuff */ view japanese
2805    if (!found) {
2806        ap_module_symbol_t *current = ap_prelinked_module_symbols;
2807
2808        for (; current->name; ++current) {
2809            if (!strcmp(current->name, name)) {
2810                found = current->modp;
2811                break;
2812            }
2813        }
2814    }
2815
2816    /* search dynamic stuff */ view japanese
2817    if (!found) {
2818        APR_OPTIONAL_FN_TYPE(ap_find_loaded_module_symbol) *check_symbol =
2819            APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_find_loaded_module_symbol);
2820
2821        if (check_symbol) {
2822            /*
2823             * There are two phases where calling ap_find_loaded_module_symbol
2824             * is problematic:
2825             *
2826             * During reading of the config, ap_server_conf is invalid but s
2827             * points to the main server config, if passed from cmd->server
2828             * of an EXEC_ON_READ directive.
2829             *
2830             * During config parsing, s may be a virtual host that would cause
2831             * a segfault in mod_so if passed to ap_find_loaded_module_symbol,
2832             * because mod_so's server config for vhosts is initialized later.
2833             * But ap_server_conf is already set at this time.
2834             *
2835             * Therefore we use s if it is not virtual and ap_server_conf if
2836             * s is virtual.
2837             */ view japanese
2838            found = check_symbol(s->is_virtual ? ap_server_conf : s, name);
2839        }
2840    }
2841
2842    return found;
2843}
2844
2845/* Callback function type used by start_cond_section. */ view japanese
2846typedef int (*test_cond_section_fn)(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *arg);
2847
2848/* Implementation of <IfXXXXX>-style conditional sections.  Callback
2849 * to test condition must be in cmd->info, matching function type
2850 * test_cond_section_fn. */ view japanese
2851static const char *start_cond_section(cmd_parms *cmd, void *mconfig, const char *arg)
2852{
2853    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2854    int result, not = (arg[0] == '!');
2855    test_cond_section_fn testfn = (test_cond_section_fn)cmd->info;
2856    const char *arg1;
2857
2858    if (endp == NULL) {
2859        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2860    }
2861
2862    arg = apr_pstrmemdup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2863
2864    if (not) {
2865        arg++;
2866    }
2867
2868    arg1 = ap_getword_conf(cmd->temp_pool, &arg);
2869
2870    if (!arg1[0]) {
2871        return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2872    }
2873
2874    result = testfn(cmd, arg1);
2875
2876    if ((!not && result) || (not && !result)) {
2877        ap_directive_t *parent = NULL;
2878        ap_directive_t *current = NULL;
2879        const char *retval;
2880
2881        retval = ap_build_cont_config(cmd->pool, cmd->temp_pool, cmd,
2882                                      &current, &parent, (char *)cmd->cmd->name);
2883        *(ap_directive_t **)mconfig = current;
2884        return retval;
2885    }
2886    else {
2887        *(ap_directive_t **)mconfig = NULL;
2888        return ap_soak_end_container(cmd, (char *)cmd->cmd->name);
2889    }
2890}
2891
2892/* Callback to implement <IfModule> test for start_cond_section. */ view japanese
2893static int test_ifmod_section(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *arg)
2894{
2895    return find_module(cmd->server, arg) != NULL;
2896}
2897
2898AP_DECLARE(int) ap_exists_config_define(const char *name)
2899{
2900    return ap_array_str_contains(ap_server_config_defines, name);
2901}
2902
2903static int test_ifdefine_section(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *arg)
2904{
2905    return ap_exists_config_define(arg);
2906}
2907
2908static int test_iffile_section(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *arg)
2909{
2910    const char *relative;
2911    apr_finfo_t sb;
2912
2913    /*
2914     * At least on Windows, if the path we are testing is not valid (for example
2915     * a path on a USB key that is not plugged), 'ap_server_root_relative()' will
2916     * return NULL. In such a case, consider that the file is not there and that
2917     * the section should be skipped.
2918     */ view japanese
2919    relative = ap_server_root_relative(cmd->temp_pool, arg);
2920    return (relative &&
2921           (apr_stat(&sb, relative, APR_FINFO_TYPE, cmd->temp_pool) == APR_SUCCESS));
2922}
2923
2924static int test_ifdirective_section(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *arg)
2925{
2926    return ap_exists_directive(cmd->temp_pool, arg);
2927}
2928
2929static int test_ifsection_section(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *arg)
2930{
2931    const char *name = apr_pstrcat(cmd->temp_pool, "<", arg, NULL);
2932    return ap_exists_directive(cmd->temp_pool, name);
2933}
2934
2935/* httpd.conf commands... beginning with the <VirtualHost> business */ view japanese
2936
2937static const char *virtualhost_section(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
2938                                       const char *arg)
2939{
2940    server_rec *main_server = cmd->server, *s;
2941    const char *errmsg;
2942    const char *endp = ap_strrchr_c(arg, '>');
2943    apr_pool_t *p = cmd->pool;
2944
2945    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
2946    if (err != NULL) {
2947        return err;
2948    }
2949
2950    if (endp == NULL) {
2951        return unclosed_directive(cmd);
2952    }
2953
2954    arg = apr_pstrndup(cmd->temp_pool, arg, endp - arg);
2955
2956    if (!arg[0]) {
2957        return missing_container_arg(cmd);
2958    }
2959
2960    /* FIXME: There's another feature waiting to happen here -- since you
2961        can now put multiple addresses/names on a single <VirtualHost>
2962        you might want to use it to group common definitions and then
2963        define other "subhosts" with their individual differences.  But
2964        personally I'd rather just do it with a macro preprocessor. -djg */ view japanese
2965    if (main_server->is_virtual) {
2966        return "<VirtualHost> doesn't nest!";
2967    }
2968
2969    errmsg = ap_init_virtual_host(p, arg, main_server, &s);
2970    if (errmsg) {
2971        return errmsg;
2972    }
2973
2974    s->next = main_server->next;
2975    main_server->next = s;
2976
2977    s->defn_name = cmd->directive->filename;
2978    s->defn_line_number = cmd->directive->line_num;
2979
2980    cmd->server = s;
2981
2982    errmsg = ap_walk_config(cmd->directive->first_child, cmd,
2983                            s->lookup_defaults);
2984
2985    cmd->server = main_server;
2986
2987    return errmsg;
2988}
2989
2990static const char *set_regex_default_options(cmd_parms *cmd,
2991                                             void *dummy,
2992                                             const char *arg)
2993{
2994    const command_rec *thiscmd = cmd->cmd;
2995    int cflags, cflag;
2996
2997    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
2998    if (err != NULL) {
2999        return err;
3000    }
3001
3002    cflags = ap_regcomp_get_default_cflags();
3003    while (*arg) {
3004        const char *name = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
3005        int how = 0;
3006
3007        if (strcasecmp(name, "none") == 0) {
3008            cflags = 0;
3009            continue;
3010        }
3011
3012        if (*name == '+') {
3013            name++;
3014            how = +1;
3015        }
3016        else if (*name == '-') {
3017            name++;
3018            how = -1;
3019        }
3020
3021        cflag = ap_regcomp_default_cflag_by_name(name);
3022        if (!cflag) {
3023            return apr_psprintf(cmd->pool, "%s: option '%s' unknown",
3024                                thiscmd->name, name);
3025        }
3026
3027        if (how > 0) {
3028            cflags |= cflag;
3029        }
3030        else if (how < 0) {
3031            cflags &= ~cflag;
3032        }
3033        else {
3034            cflags = cflag;
3035        }
3036    }
3037    ap_regcomp_set_default_cflags(cflags);
3038
3039    return NULL;
3040}
3041
3042static const char *set_server_alias(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3043                                    const char *arg)
3044{
3045    if (!cmd->server->names) {
3046        return "ServerAlias only used in <VirtualHost>";
3047    }
3048
3049    while (*arg) {
3050        char **item, *name = ap_getword_conf(cmd->pool, &arg);
3051
3052        if (ap_is_matchexp(name)) {
3053            item = (char **)apr_array_push(cmd->server->wild_names);
3054        }
3055        else {
3056            item = (char **)apr_array_push(cmd->server->names);
3057        }
3058
3059        *item = name;
3060    }
3061
3062    return NULL;
3063}
3064
3065static const char *set_accf_map(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3066                                const char *iproto, const char* iaccf)
3067{
3068    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
3069    core_server_config *conf =
3070        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
3071    char* proto;
3072    char* accf;
3073    if (err != NULL) {
3074        return err;
3075    }
3076
3077    proto = apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, iproto);
3078    ap_str_tolower(proto);
3079    accf = apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, iaccf);
3080    ap_str_tolower(accf);
3081    apr_table_setn(conf->accf_map, proto, accf);
3082
3083    return NULL;
3084}
3085
3086AP_DECLARE(const char*) ap_get_server_protocol(server_rec* s)
3087{
3088    core_server_config *conf = ap_get_core_module_config(s->module_config);
3089    return conf->protocol;
3090}
3091
3092AP_DECLARE(void) ap_set_server_protocol(server_rec* s, const char* proto)
3093{
3094    core_server_config *conf = ap_get_core_module_config(s->module_config);
3095    conf->protocol = proto;
3096}
3097
3098static const char *set_protocol(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3099                                const char *arg)
3100{
3101    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3102    core_server_config *conf =
3103        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
3104    char* proto;
3105
3106    if (err != NULL) {
3107        return err;
3108    }
3109
3110    proto = apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, arg);
3111    ap_str_tolower(proto);
3112    conf->protocol = proto;
3113
3114    return NULL;
3115}
3116
3117static const char *set_server_string_slot(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3118                                          const char *arg)
3119{
3120    /* This one's pretty generic... */ view japanese
3121
3122    int offset = (int)(long)cmd->info;
3123    char *struct_ptr = (char *)cmd->server;
3124
3125    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3126    if (err != NULL) {
3127        return err;
3128    }
3129
3130    *(const char **)(struct_ptr + offset) = arg;
3131    return NULL;
3132}
3133
3134/*
3135 * The ServerName directive takes one argument with format
3136 * [scheme://]fully-qualified-domain-name[:port], for instance
3137 * ServerName www.example.com
3138 * ServerName www.example.com:80
3139 * ServerName https://www.example.com:443
3140 */ view japanese
3141
3142static const char *server_hostname_port(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy, const char *arg)
3143{
3144    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3145    const char *portstr, *part;
3146    char *scheme;
3147    int port;
3148
3149    if (err != NULL) {
3150        return err;
3151    }
3152
3153    if (apr_fnmatch_test(arg))
3154        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->temp_pool, "Invalid ServerName \"", arg,
3155                "\" use ServerAlias to set multiple server names.", NULL);
3156
3157    part = ap_strstr_c(arg, "://");
3158
3159    if (part) {
3160      scheme = apr_pstrndup(cmd->pool, arg, part - arg);
3161      ap_str_tolower(scheme);
3162      cmd->server->server_scheme = (const char *)scheme;
3163      part += 3;
3164    } else {
3165      part = arg;
3166    }
3167
3168    portstr = ap_strchr_c(part, ':');
3169    if (portstr) {
3170        cmd->server->server_hostname = apr_pstrndup(cmd->pool, part,
3171                                                    portstr - part);
3172        portstr++;
3173        port = atoi(portstr);
3174        if (port <= 0 || port >= 65536) { /* 65536 == 1<<16 */ view japanese
3175            return apr_pstrcat(cmd->temp_pool, "The port number \"", arg,
3176                          "\" is outside the appropriate range "
3177                          "(i.e., 1..65535).", NULL);
3178        }
3179    }
3180    else {
3181        cmd->server->server_hostname = apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, part);
3182        port = 0;
3183    }
3184
3185    cmd->server->port = port;
3186    return NULL;
3187}
3188
3189static const char *set_signature_flag(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
3190                                      const char *arg)
3191{
3192    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3193
3194    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "On") == 0) {
3195        d->server_signature = srv_sig_on;
3196    }
3197    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Off") == 0) {
3198        d->server_signature = srv_sig_off;
3199    }
3200    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "EMail") == 0) {
3201        d->server_signature = srv_sig_withmail;
3202    }
3203    else {
3204        return "ServerSignature: use one of: off | on | email";
3205    }
3206
3207    return NULL;
3208}
3209
3210static const char *set_server_root(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3211                                   const char *arg)
3212{
3213    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
3214
3215    if (err != NULL) {
3216        return err;
3217    }
3218
3219    if ((apr_filepath_merge((char**)&ap_server_root, NULL, arg,
3220                            APR_FILEPATH_TRUENAME, cmd->pool) != APR_SUCCESS)
3221        || !ap_is_directory(cmd->temp_pool, ap_server_root)) {
3222        return "ServerRoot must be a valid directory";
3223    }
3224
3225    return NULL;
3226}
3227
3228static const char *set_runtime_dir(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy, const char *arg)
3229{
3230    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
3231
3232    if (err != NULL) {
3233        return err;
3234    }
3235
3236    if ((apr_filepath_merge((char**)&ap_runtime_dir, NULL,
3237                            ap_server_root_relative(cmd->temp_pool, arg),
3238                            APR_FILEPATH_TRUENAME, cmd->pool) != APR_SUCCESS)
3239        || !ap_is_directory(cmd->temp_pool, ap_runtime_dir)) {
3240        return "DefaultRuntimeDir must be a valid directory, absolute or relative to ServerRoot";
3241    }
3242
3243    return NULL;
3244}
3245
3246static const char *set_timeout(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy, const char *arg)
3247{
3248    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3249
3250    if (err != NULL) {
3251        return err;
3252    }
3253
3254    cmd->server->timeout = apr_time_from_sec(atoi(arg));
3255    return NULL;
3256}
3257
3258static const char *set_allow2f(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_, const char *arg)
3259{
3260    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3261
3262    if (0 == ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on")) {
3263        d->allow_encoded_slashes = 1;
3264        d->decode_encoded_slashes = 1; /* for compatibility with 2.0 & 2.2 */ view japanese
3265    } else if (0 == ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off")) {
3266        d->allow_encoded_slashes = 0;
3267        d->decode_encoded_slashes = 0;
3268    } else if (0 == ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "nodecode")) {
3269        d->allow_encoded_slashes = 1;
3270        d->decode_encoded_slashes = 0;
3271    } else {
3272        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool,
3273                           cmd->cmd->name, " must be On, Off, or NoDecode",
3274                           NULL);
3275    }
3276    return NULL;
3277}
3278
3279static const char *set_hostname_lookups(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
3280                                        const char *arg)
3281{
3282    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3283
3284    if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on")) {
3285        d->hostname_lookups = HOSTNAME_LOOKUP_ON;
3286    }
3287    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off")) {
3288        d->hostname_lookups = HOSTNAME_LOOKUP_OFF;
3289    }
3290    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "double")) {
3291        d->hostname_lookups = HOSTNAME_LOOKUP_DOUBLE;
3292    }
3293    else {
3294        return "parameter must be 'on', 'off', or 'double'";
3295    }
3296
3297    return NULL;
3298}
3299
3300static const char *set_serverpath(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3301                                  const char *arg)
3302{
3303    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3304
3305    if (err != NULL) {
3306        return err;
3307    }
3308
3309    cmd->server->path = arg;
3310    cmd->server->pathlen = (int)strlen(arg);
3311    return NULL;
3312}
3313
3314static const char *set_content_md5(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_, int arg)
3315{
3316    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3317
3318    d->content_md5 = arg ? AP_CONTENT_MD5_ON : AP_CONTENT_MD5_OFF;
3319    return NULL;
3320}
3321
3322static const char *set_accept_path_info(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_, const char *arg)
3323{
3324    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3325
3326    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on") == 0) {
3327        d->accept_path_info = AP_REQ_ACCEPT_PATH_INFO;
3328    }
3329    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off") == 0) {
3330        d->accept_path_info = AP_REQ_REJECT_PATH_INFO;
3331    }
3332    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "default") == 0) {
3333        d->accept_path_info = AP_REQ_DEFAULT_PATH_INFO;
3334    }
3335    else {
3336        return "AcceptPathInfo must be set to on, off or default";
3337    }
3338
3339    return NULL;
3340}
3341
3342static const char *set_use_canonical_name(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
3343                                          const char *arg)
3344{
3345    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3346
3347    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on") == 0) {
3348        d->use_canonical_name = USE_CANONICAL_NAME_ON;
3349    }
3350    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off") == 0) {
3351        d->use_canonical_name = USE_CANONICAL_NAME_OFF;
3352    }
3353    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "dns") == 0) {
3354        d->use_canonical_name = USE_CANONICAL_NAME_DNS;
3355    }
3356    else {
3357        return "parameter must be 'on', 'off', or 'dns'";
3358    }
3359
3360    return NULL;
3361}
3362
3363static const char *set_use_canonical_phys_port(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_,
3364                                          const char *arg)
3365{
3366    core_dir_config *d = d_;
3367
3368    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on") == 0) {
3369        d->use_canonical_phys_port = USE_CANONICAL_PHYS_PORT_ON;
3370    }
3371    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off") == 0) {
3372        d->use_canonical_phys_port = USE_CANONICAL_PHYS_PORT_OFF;
3373    }
3374    else {
3375        return "parameter must be 'on' or 'off'";
3376    }
3377
3378    return NULL;
3379}
3380
3381static const char *include_config (cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3382                                   const char *name)
3383{
3384    ap_directive_t *conftree = NULL;
3385    const char *conffile, *error;
3386    unsigned *recursion;
3387    int optional = cmd->cmd->cmd_data ? 1 : 0;
3388    void *data;
3389
3390    /* NOTE: ap_include_sentinel is also used by ap_process_resource_config()
3391     * during DUMP_INCLUDES; don't change its type or remove it without updating
3392     * the other.
3393     */ view japanese
3394    apr_pool_userdata_get(&data, "ap_include_sentinel", cmd->pool);
3395    if (data) {
3396        recursion = data;
3397    }
3398    else {
3399        data = recursion = apr_palloc(cmd->pool, sizeof(*recursion));
3400        *recursion = 0;
3401        apr_pool_userdata_setn(data, "ap_include_sentinel", NULL, cmd->pool);
3402    }
3403
3404    if (++*recursion > AP_MAX_INCLUDE_DEPTH) {
3405        *recursion = 0;
3406        return apr_psprintf(cmd->pool, "Exceeded maximum include depth of %u, "
3407                            "There appears to be a recursion.",
3408                            AP_MAX_INCLUDE_DEPTH);
3409    }
3410
3411    conffile = ap_server_root_relative(cmd->pool, name);
3412    if (!conffile) {
3413        *recursion = 0;
3414        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->pool, "Invalid Include path ",
3415                           name, NULL);
3416    }
3417
3418    if (ap_exists_config_define("DUMP_INCLUDES")) {
3419        unsigned *line_number;
3420
3421        /* NOTE: ap_include_lineno is used by ap_process_resource_config()
3422         * during DUMP_INCLUDES; don't change its type or remove it without
3423         * updating the other.
3424         */ view japanese
3425        apr_pool_userdata_get(&data, "ap_include_lineno", cmd->pool);
3426        if (data) {
3427            line_number = data;
3428        } else {
3429            data = line_number = apr_palloc(cmd->pool, sizeof(*line_number));
3430            apr_pool_userdata_setn(data, "ap_include_lineno", NULL, cmd->pool);
3431        }
3432
3433        *line_number = cmd->config_file->line_number;
3434    }
3435
3436    error = ap_process_fnmatch_configs(cmd->server, conffile, &conftree,
3437                                       cmd->pool, cmd->temp_pool,
3438                                       optional);
3439    if (error) {
3440        *recursion = 0;
3441        return error;
3442    }
3443
3444    *(ap_directive_t **)dummy = conftree;
3445
3446    /* recursion level done */ view japanese
3447    if (*recursion) {
3448        --*recursion;
3449    }
3450
3451    return NULL;
3452}
3453
3454static const char *set_loglevel(cmd_parms *cmd, void *config_, const char *arg_)
3455{
3456    char *level_str;
3457    int level;
3458    module *module;
3459    char *arg = apr_pstrdup(cmd->temp_pool, arg_);
3460    struct ap_logconf *log;
3461    const char *err;
3462
3463    if (cmd->path) {
3464        core_dir_config *dconf = config_;
3465        if (!dconf->log) {
3466            dconf->log = ap_new_log_config(cmd->pool, NULL);
3467        }
3468        log = dconf->log;
3469    }
3470    else {
3471        log = &cmd->server->log;
3472    }
3473
3474    if (arg == NULL)
3475        return "LogLevel requires level keyword or module loglevel specifier";
3476
3477    level_str = ap_strrchr(arg, ':');
3478
3479    if (level_str == NULL) {
3480        err = ap_parse_log_level(arg, &log->level);
3481        if (err != NULL)
3482            return err;
3483        ap_reset_module_loglevels(log, APLOG_NO_MODULE);
3484        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE3, 0, cmd->server,
3485                     "Setting LogLevel for all modules to %s", arg);
3486        return NULL;
3487    }
3488
3489    *level_str++ = '\0';
3490    if (!*level_str) {
3491        return apr_psprintf(cmd->temp_pool, "Module specifier '%s' must be "
3492                            "followed by a log level keyword", arg);
3493    }
3494
3495    err = ap_parse_log_level(level_str, &level);
3496    if (err != NULL)
3497        return apr_psprintf(cmd->temp_pool, "%s:%s: %s", arg, level_str, err);
3498
3499    if ((module = find_module(cmd->server, arg)) == NULL) {
3500        char *name = apr_psprintf(cmd->temp_pool, "%s_module", arg);
3501        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE6, 0, cmd->server,
3502                     "Cannot find module '%s', trying '%s'", arg, name);
3503        module = find_module(cmd->server, name);
3504    }
3505
3506    if (module == NULL) {
3507        return apr_psprintf(cmd->temp_pool, "Cannot find module %s", arg);
3508    }
3509
3510    ap_set_module_loglevel(cmd->pool, log, module->module_index, level);
3511    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE3, 0, cmd->server,
3512                 "Setting LogLevel for module %s to %s", module->name,
3513                 level_str);
3514
3515    return NULL;
3516}
3517
3518AP_DECLARE(const char *) ap_psignature(const char *prefix, request_rec *r)
3519{
3520    char sport[20];
3521    core_dir_config *conf;
3522
3523    conf = (core_dir_config *)ap_get_core_module_config(r->per_dir_config);
3524    if ((conf->server_signature == srv_sig_off)
3525            || (conf->server_signature == srv_sig_unset)) {
3526        return "";
3527    }
3528
3529    apr_snprintf(sport, sizeof sport, "%u", (unsigned) ap_get_server_port(r));
3530
3531    if (conf->server_signature == srv_sig_withmail) {
3532        return apr_pstrcat(r->pool, prefix, "<address>",
3533                           ap_get_server_banner(),
3534                           " Server at <a href=\"",
3535                           ap_is_url(r->server->server_admin) ? "" : "mailto:",
3536                           ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->server->server_admin),
3537                           "\">",
3538                           ap_escape_html(r->pool, ap_get_server_name(r)),
3539                           "</a> Port ", sport,
3540                           "</address>\n", NULL);
3541    }
3542
3543    return apr_pstrcat(r->pool, prefix, "<address>", ap_get_server_banner(),
3544                       " Server at ",
3545                       ap_escape_html(r->pool, ap_get_server_name(r)),
3546                       " Port ", sport,
3547                       "</address>\n", NULL);
3548}
3549
3550/*
3551 * Handle a request to include the server's OS platform in the Server
3552 * response header field (the ServerTokens directive).  Unfortunately
3553 * this requires a new global in order to communicate the setting back to
3554 * http_main so it can insert the information in the right place in the
3555 * string.
3556 */ view japanese
3557
3558static char *server_banner = NULL;
3559static int banner_locked = 0;
3560static const char *server_description = NULL;
3561
3562enum server_token_type {
3563    SrvTk_MAJOR,         /* eg: Apache/2 */ view japanese
3564    SrvTk_MINOR,         /* eg. Apache/2.0 */ view japanese
3565    SrvTk_MINIMAL,       /* eg: Apache/2.0.41 */ view japanese
3566    SrvTk_OS,            /* eg: Apache/2.0.41 (UNIX) */ view japanese
3567    SrvTk_FULL,          /* eg: Apache/2.0.41 (UNIX) PHP/4.2.2 FooBar/1.2b */ view japanese
3568    SrvTk_PRODUCT_ONLY   /* eg: Apache */ view japanese
3569};
3570static enum server_token_type ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_FULL;
3571
3572static apr_status_t reset_banner(void *dummy)
3573{
3574    banner_locked = 0;
3575    ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_FULL;
3576    server_banner = NULL;
3577    server_description = NULL;
3578    return APR_SUCCESS;
3579}
3580
3581AP_DECLARE(void) ap_get_server_revision(ap_version_t *version)
3582{
3583    version->major = AP_SERVER_MAJORVERSION_NUMBER;
3584    version->minor = AP_SERVER_MINORVERSION_NUMBER;
3585    version->patch = AP_SERVER_PATCHLEVEL_NUMBER;
3586    version->add_string = AP_SERVER_ADD_STRING;
3587}
3588
3589AP_DECLARE(const char *) ap_get_server_description(void)
3590{
3591    return server_description ? server_description :
3592        AP_SERVER_BASEVERSION " (" PLATFORM ")";
3593}
3594
3595AP_DECLARE(const char *) ap_get_server_banner(void)
3596{
3597    return server_banner ? server_banner : AP_SERVER_BASEVERSION;
3598}
3599
3600AP_DECLARE(void) ap_add_version_component(apr_pool_t *pconf, const char *component)
3601{
3602    if (! banner_locked) {
3603        /*
3604         * If the version string is null, register our cleanup to reset the
3605         * pointer on pool destruction. We also know that, if NULL,
3606         * we are adding the original SERVER_BASEVERSION string.
3607         */ view japanese
3608        if (server_banner == NULL) {
3609            apr_pool_cleanup_register(pconf, NULL, reset_banner,
3610                                      apr_pool_cleanup_null);
3611            server_banner = apr_pstrdup(pconf, component);
3612        }
3613        else {
3614            /*
3615             * Tack the given component identifier to the end of
3616             * the existing string.
3617             */ view japanese
3618            server_banner = apr_pstrcat(pconf, server_banner, " ",
3619                                        component, NULL);
3620        }
3621    }
3622    server_description = apr_pstrcat(pconf, server_description, " ",
3623                                     component, NULL);
3624}
3625
3626/*
3627 * This routine adds the real server base identity to the banner string,
3628 * and then locks out changes until the next reconfig.
3629 */ view japanese
3630static void set_banner(apr_pool_t *pconf)
3631{
3632    if (ap_server_tokens == SrvTk_PRODUCT_ONLY) {
3633        ap_add_version_component(pconf, AP_SERVER_BASEPRODUCT);
3634    }
3635    else if (ap_server_tokens == SrvTk_MINIMAL) {
3636        ap_add_version_component(pconf, AP_SERVER_BASEVERSION);
3637    }
3638    else if (ap_server_tokens == SrvTk_MINOR) {
3639        ap_add_version_component(pconf, AP_SERVER_BASEPRODUCT "/" AP_SERVER_MINORREVISION);
3640    }
3641    else if (ap_server_tokens == SrvTk_MAJOR) {
3642        ap_add_version_component(pconf, AP_SERVER_BASEPRODUCT "/" AP_SERVER_MAJORVERSION);
3643    }
3644    else {
3645        ap_add_version_component(pconf, AP_SERVER_BASEVERSION " (" PLATFORM ")");
3646    }
3647
3648    /*
3649     * Lock the server_banner string if we're not displaying
3650     * the full set of tokens
3651     */ view japanese
3652    if (ap_server_tokens != SrvTk_FULL) {
3653        banner_locked++;
3654    }
3655    server_description = AP_SERVER_BASEVERSION " (" PLATFORM ")";
3656}
3657
3658static const char *set_serv_tokens(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3659                                   const char *arg)
3660{
3661    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
3662
3663    if (err != NULL) {
3664        return err;
3665    }
3666
3667    if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "OS")) {
3668        ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_OS;
3669    }
3670    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Min") || !ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Minimal")) {
3671        ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_MINIMAL;
3672    }
3673    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Major")) {
3674        ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_MAJOR;
3675    }
3676    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Minor") ) {
3677        ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_MINOR;
3678    }
3679    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Prod") || !ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "ProductOnly")) {
3680        ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_PRODUCT_ONLY;
3681    }
3682    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "Full")) {
3683        ap_server_tokens = SrvTk_FULL;
3684    }
3685    else {
3686        return "ServerTokens takes 1 argument: 'Prod(uctOnly)', 'Major', 'Minor', 'Min(imal)', 'OS', or 'Full'";
3687    }
3688
3689    return NULL;
3690}
3691
3692static const char *set_limit_req_line(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3693                                      const char *arg)
3694{
3695    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3696    int lim;
3697
3698    if (err != NULL) {
3699        return err;
3700    }
3701
3702    lim = atoi(arg);
3703    if (lim < 0) {
3704        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->temp_pool, "LimitRequestLine \"", arg,
3705                           "\" must be a non-negative integer", NULL);
3706    }
3707
3708    cmd->server->limit_req_line = lim;
3709    return NULL;
3710}
3711
3712static const char *set_limit_req_fieldsize(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3713                                           const char *arg)
3714{
3715    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3716    int lim;
3717
3718    if (err != NULL) {
3719        return err;
3720    }
3721
3722    lim = atoi(arg);
3723    if (lim < 0) {
3724        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->temp_pool, "LimitRequestFieldsize \"", arg,
3725                          "\" must be a non-negative integer",
3726                          NULL);
3727    }
3728
3729    cmd->server->limit_req_fieldsize = lim;
3730    return NULL;
3731}
3732
3733static const char *set_limit_req_fields(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3734                                        const char *arg)
3735{
3736    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
3737    int lim;
3738
3739    if (err != NULL) {
3740        return err;
3741    }
3742
3743    lim = atoi(arg);
3744    if (lim < 0) {
3745        return apr_pstrcat(cmd->temp_pool, "LimitRequestFields \"", arg,
3746                           "\" must be a non-negative integer (0 = no limit)",
3747                           NULL);
3748    }
3749
3750    cmd->server->limit_req_fields = lim;
3751    return NULL;
3752}
3753
3754static const char *set_limit_req_body(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_,
3755                                      const char *arg)
3756{
3757    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3758    char *errp;
3759
3760    if (APR_SUCCESS != apr_strtoff(&conf->limit_req_body, arg, &errp, 10)) {
3761        return "LimitRequestBody argument is not parsable.";
3762    }
3763    if (*errp || conf->limit_req_body < 0) {
3764        return "LimitRequestBody requires a non-negative integer.";
3765    }
3766
3767    return NULL;
3768}
3769
3770static const char *set_limit_xml_req_body(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_,
3771                                          const char *arg)
3772{
3773    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3774
3775    conf->limit_xml_body = atol(arg);
3776    if (conf->limit_xml_body < 0)
3777        return "LimitXMLRequestBody requires a non-negative integer.";
3778
3779    /* zero is AP_MAX_LIMIT_XML_BODY (implicitly) */ view japanese
3780    if ((apr_size_t)conf->limit_xml_body > AP_MAX_LIMIT_XML_BODY)
3781        return apr_psprintf(cmd->pool, "LimitXMLRequestBody must not exceed "
3782                            "%" APR_SIZE_T_FMT, AP_MAX_LIMIT_XML_BODY);
3783
3784    return NULL;
3785}
3786
3787static const char *set_max_ranges(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_, const char *arg)
3788{
3789    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3790    int val = 0;
3791
3792    if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "none")) {
3793        val = AP_MAXRANGES_NORANGES;
3794    }
3795    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "default")) {
3796        val = AP_MAXRANGES_DEFAULT;
3797    }
3798    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "unlimited")) {
3799        val = AP_MAXRANGES_UNLIMITED;
3800    }
3801    else {
3802        val = atoi(arg);
3803        if (val <= 0)
3804            return "MaxRanges requires 'none', 'default', 'unlimited' or "
3805                   "a positive integer";
3806    }
3807
3808    conf->max_ranges = val;
3809
3810    return NULL;
3811}
3812
3813static const char *set_max_overlaps(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_, const char *arg)
3814{
3815    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3816    int val = 0;
3817
3818    if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "none")) {
3819        val = AP_MAXRANGES_NORANGES;
3820    }
3821    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "default")) {
3822        val = AP_MAXRANGES_DEFAULT;
3823    }
3824    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "unlimited")) {
3825        val = AP_MAXRANGES_UNLIMITED;
3826    }
3827    else {
3828        val = atoi(arg);
3829        if (val <= 0)
3830            return "MaxRangeOverlaps requires 'none', 'default', 'unlimited' or "
3831            "a positive integer";
3832    }
3833
3834    conf->max_overlaps = val;
3835
3836    return NULL;
3837}
3838
3839static const char *set_max_reversals(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_, const char *arg)
3840{
3841    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3842    int val = 0;
3843
3844    if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "none")) {
3845        val = AP_MAXRANGES_NORANGES;
3846    }
3847    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "default")) {
3848        val = AP_MAXRANGES_DEFAULT;
3849    }
3850    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "unlimited")) {
3851        val = AP_MAXRANGES_UNLIMITED;
3852    }
3853    else {
3854        val = atoi(arg);
3855        if (val <= 0)
3856            return "MaxRangeReversals requires 'none', 'default', 'unlimited' or "
3857            "a positive integer";
3858    }
3859
3860    conf->max_reversals = val;
3861
3862    return NULL;
3863}
3864
3865AP_DECLARE(apr_size_t) ap_get_limit_xml_body(const request_rec *r)
3866{
3867    core_dir_config *conf;
3868
3869    conf = ap_get_core_module_config(r->per_dir_config);
3870    if (conf->limit_xml_body == AP_LIMIT_UNSET)
3871        return AP_DEFAULT_LIMIT_XML_BODY;
3872    if (conf->limit_xml_body == 0)
3873        return AP_MAX_LIMIT_XML_BODY;
3874
3875    return (apr_size_t)conf->limit_xml_body;
3876}
3877
3878#if !defined (RLIMIT_CPU) || !(defined (RLIMIT_DATA) || defined (RLIMIT_VMEM) || defined(RLIMIT_AS)) || !defined (RLIMIT_NPROC)
3879static const char *no_set_limit(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_,
3880                                const char *arg, const char *arg2)
3881{
3882    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00118)
3883                "%s not supported on this platform", cmd->cmd->name);
3884
3885    return NULL;
3886}
3887#endif
3888
3889#ifdef RLIMIT_CPU
3890static const char *set_limit_cpu(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_,
3891                                 const char *arg, const char *arg2)
3892{
3893    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3894
3895    ap_unixd_set_rlimit(cmd, &conf->limit_cpu, arg, arg2, RLIMIT_CPU);
3896    return NULL;
3897}
3898#endif
3899
3900#if defined (RLIMIT_DATA) || defined (RLIMIT_VMEM) || defined(RLIMIT_AS)
3901static const char *set_limit_mem(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_,
3902                                 const char *arg, const char * arg2)
3903{
3904    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3905
3906#if defined(RLIMIT_AS)
3907    ap_unixd_set_rlimit(cmd, &conf->limit_mem, arg, arg2 ,RLIMIT_AS);
3908#elif defined(RLIMIT_DATA)
3909    ap_unixd_set_rlimit(cmd, &conf->limit_mem, arg, arg2, RLIMIT_DATA);
3910#elif defined(RLIMIT_VMEM)
3911    ap_unixd_set_rlimit(cmd, &conf->limit_mem, arg, arg2, RLIMIT_VMEM);
3912#endif
3913
3914    return NULL;
3915}
3916#endif
3917
3918#ifdef RLIMIT_NPROC
3919static const char *set_limit_nproc(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf_,
3920                                   const char *arg, const char * arg2)
3921{
3922    core_dir_config *conf = conf_;
3923
3924    ap_unixd_set_rlimit(cmd, &conf->limit_nproc, arg, arg2, RLIMIT_NPROC);
3925    return NULL;
3926}
3927#endif
3928
3929static const char *set_recursion_limit(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
3930                                       const char *arg1, const char *arg2)
3931{
3932    core_server_config *conf =
3933        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
3934    int limit = atoi(arg1);
3935
3936    if (limit <= 0) {
3937        return "The recursion limit must be greater than zero.";
3938    }
3939    if (limit < 4) {
3940        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00119)
3941                     "Limiting internal redirects to very low numbers may "
3942                     "cause normal requests to fail.");
3943    }
3944
3945    conf->redirect_limit = limit;
3946
3947    if (arg2) {
3948        limit = atoi(arg2);
3949
3950        if (limit <= 0) {
3951            return "The recursion limit must be greater than zero.";
3952        }
3953        if (limit < 4) {
3954            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00120)
3955                         "Limiting the subrequest depth to a very low level may"
3956                         " cause normal requests to fail.");
3957        }
3958    }
3959
3960    conf->subreq_limit = limit;
3961
3962    return NULL;
3963}
3964
3965static void log_backtrace(const request_rec *r)
3966{
3967    if (APLOGrdebug(r)) {
3968        const request_rec *top = r;
3969
3970        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00121)
3971                      "r->uri = %s", r->uri ? r->uri : "(unexpectedly NULL)");
3972
3973        while (top && (top->prev || top->main)) {
3974            if (top->prev) {
3975                top = top->prev;
3976                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00122)
3977                              "redirected from r->uri = %s",
3978                              top->uri ? top->uri : "(unexpectedly NULL)");
3979            }
3980
3981            if (!top->prev && top->main) {
3982                top = top->main;
3983                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00123)
3984                              "subrequested from r->uri = %s",
3985                              top->uri ? top->uri : "(unexpectedly NULL)");
3986            }
3987        }
3988    }
3989}
3990
3991/*
3992 * check whether redirect limit is reached
3993 */ view japanese
3994AP_DECLARE(int) ap_is_recursion_limit_exceeded(const request_rec *r)
3995{
3996    core_server_config *conf =
3997        ap_get_core_module_config(r->server->module_config);
3998    const request_rec *top = r;
3999    int redirects = 0, subreqs = 0;
4000    int rlimit = conf->redirect_limit
4001                 ? conf->redirect_limit
4002                 : AP_DEFAULT_MAX_INTERNAL_REDIRECTS;
4003    int slimit = conf->subreq_limit
4004                 ? conf->subreq_limit
4005                 : AP_DEFAULT_MAX_SUBREQ_DEPTH;
4006
4007
4008    while (top->prev || top->main) {
4009        if (top->prev) {
4010            if (++redirects >= rlimit) {
4011                /* uuh, too much. */
4012                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00124)
4013                              "Request exceeded the limit of %d internal "
4014                              "redirects due to probable configuration error. "
4015                              "Use 'LimitInternalRecursion' to increase the "
4016                              "limit if necessary. Use 'LogLevel debug' to get "
4017                              "a backtrace.", rlimit);
4018
4019                /* post backtrace */
4020                log_backtrace(r);
4021
4022                /* return failure */
4023                return 1;
4024            }
4025
4026            top = top->prev;
4027        }
4028
4029        if (!top->prev && top->main) {
4030            if (++subreqs >= slimit) {
4031                /* uuh, too much. */ view japanese
4032                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00125)
4033                              "Request exceeded the limit of %d subrequest "
4034                              "nesting levels due to probable configuration "
4035                              "error. Use 'LimitInternalRecursion' to increase "
4036                              "the limit if necessary. Use 'LogLevel debug' to "
4037                              "get a backtrace.", slimit);
4038
4039                /* post backtrace */ view japanese
4040                log_backtrace(r);
4041
4042                /* return failure */ view japanese
4043                return 1;
4044            }
4045
4046            top = top->main;
4047        }
4048    }
4049
4050    /* recursion state: ok */ view japanese
4051    return 0;
4052}
4053
4054static const char *set_trace_enable(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
4055                                    const char *arg1)
4056{
4057    core_server_config *conf =
4058        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
4059
4060    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg1, "on") == 0) {
4061        conf->trace_enable = AP_TRACE_ENABLE;
4062    }
4063    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg1, "off") == 0) {
4064        conf->trace_enable = AP_TRACE_DISABLE;
4065    }
4066    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg1, "extended") == 0) {
4067        conf->trace_enable = AP_TRACE_EXTENDED;
4068    }
4069    else {
4070        return "TraceEnable must be one of 'on', 'off', or 'extended'";
4071    }
4072
4073    return NULL;
4074}
4075
4076static const char *set_protocols(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
4077                                 const char *arg)
4078{
4079    core_server_config *conf =
4080    ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
4081    const char **np;
4082    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
4083
4084    if (err) {
4085        return err;
4086    }
4087    
4088    np = (const char **)apr_array_push(conf->protocols);
4089    *np = arg;
4090
4091    return NULL;
4092}
4093
4094static const char *set_protocols_honor_order(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
4095                                             const char *arg)
4096{
4097    core_server_config *conf =
4098    ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
4099    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_DIR_CONTEXT);
4100    
4101    if (err) {
4102        return err;
4103    }
4104    
4105    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "on") == 0) {
4106        conf->protocols_honor_order = 1;
4107    }
4108    else if (ap_cstr_casecmp(arg, "off") == 0) {
4109        conf->protocols_honor_order = 0;
4110    }
4111    else {
4112        return "ProtocolsHonorOrder must be 'on' or 'off'";
4113    }
4114    
4115    return NULL;
4116}
4117
4118static const char *set_http_protocol_options(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
4119                                             const char *arg)
4120{
4121    core_server_config *conf =
4122        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
4123
4124    if (strcasecmp(arg, "allow0.9") == 0)
4125        conf->http09_enable |= AP_HTTP09_ENABLE;
4126    else if (strcasecmp(arg, "require1.0") == 0)
4127        conf->http09_enable |= AP_HTTP09_DISABLE;
4128    else if (strcasecmp(arg, "strict") == 0)
4129        conf->http_conformance |= AP_HTTP_CONFORMANCE_STRICT;
4130    else if (strcasecmp(arg, "unsafe") == 0)
4131        conf->http_conformance |= AP_HTTP_CONFORMANCE_UNSAFE;
4132    else if (strcasecmp(arg, "registeredmethods") == 0)
4133        conf->http_methods |= AP_HTTP_METHODS_REGISTERED;
4134    else if (strcasecmp(arg, "lenientmethods") == 0)
4135        conf->http_methods |= AP_HTTP_METHODS_LENIENT;
4136    else
4137        return "HttpProtocolOptions accepts "
4138               "'Unsafe' or 'Strict' (default), "
4139               "'RegisteredMethods' or 'LenientMethods' (default), and "
4140               "'Require1.0' or 'Allow0.9' (default)";
4141
4142    if ((conf->http09_enable & AP_HTTP09_ENABLE)
4143            && (conf->http09_enable & AP_HTTP09_DISABLE))
4144        return "HttpProtocolOptions 'Allow0.9' and 'Require1.0'"
4145               " are mutually exclusive";
4146
4147    if ((conf->http_conformance & AP_HTTP_CONFORMANCE_STRICT)
4148            && (conf->http_conformance & AP_HTTP_CONFORMANCE_UNSAFE))
4149        return "HttpProtocolOptions 'Strict' and 'Unsafe'"
4150               " are mutually exclusive";
4151
4152    if ((conf->http_methods & AP_HTTP_METHODS_REGISTERED)
4153            && (conf->http_methods & AP_HTTP_METHODS_LENIENT))
4154        return "HttpProtocolOptions 'RegisteredMethods' and 'LenientMethods'"
4155               " are mutually exclusive";
4156
4157    return NULL;
4158}
4159
4160static const char *set_http_method(cmd_parms *cmd, void *conf, const char *arg)
4161{
4162    const char *err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, GLOBAL_ONLY);
4163    if (err != NULL)
4164        return err;
4165    ap_method_register(cmd->pool, arg);
4166    return NULL;
4167}
4168
4169static apr_hash_t *errorlog_hash;
4170
4171static int log_constant_item(const ap_errorlog_info *info, const char *arg,
4172                             char *buf, int buflen)
4173{
4174    char *end = apr_cpystrn(buf, arg, buflen);
4175    return end - buf;
4176}
4177
4178static char *parse_errorlog_misc_string(apr_pool_t *p,
4179                                        ap_errorlog_format_item *it,
4180                                        const char **sa)
4181{
4182    const char *s;
4183    char scratch[MAX_STRING_LEN];
4184    char *d = scratch;
4185    /*
4186     * non-leading white space terminates this string to allow the next field
4187     * separator to be inserted
4188     */ view japanese
4189    int at_start = 1;
4190
4191    it->func = log_constant_item;
4192    s = *sa;
4193
4194    while (*s && *s != '%' && (*s != ' ' || at_start) && d < scratch + MAX_STRING_LEN) {
4195        if (*s != '\\') {
4196            if (*s != ' ') {
4197                at_start = 0;
4198            }
4199            *d++ = *s++;
4200        }
4201        else {
4202            s++;
4203            switch (*s) {
4204            case 'r':
4205                *d++ = '\r';
4206                s++;
4207                break;
4208            case 'n':
4209                *d++ = '\n';
4210                s++;
4211                break;
4212            case 't':
4213                *d++ = '\t';
4214                s++;
4215                break;
4216            case '\0':
4217                /* handle end of string */ view japanese
4218                *d++ = '\\';
4219                break;
4220            default:
4221                /* copy next char verbatim */ view japanese
4222                *d++ = *s++;
4223                break;
4224            }
4225        }
4226    }
4227    *d = '\0';
4228    it->arg = apr_pstrdup(p, scratch);
4229
4230    *sa = s;
4231    return NULL;
4232}
4233
4234static char *parse_errorlog_item(apr_pool_t *p, ap_errorlog_format_item *it,
4235                                 const char **sa)
4236{
4237    const char *s = *sa;
4238    ap_errorlog_handler *handler;
4239    int i;
4240
4241    if (*s != '%') {
4242        if (*s == ' ') {
4243            it->flags |= AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_FIELD_SEP;
4244        }
4245        return parse_errorlog_misc_string(p, it, sa);
4246    }
4247
4248    ++s;
4249
4250    if (*s == ' ') {
4251        /* percent-space (% ) is a field separator */ view japanese
4252        it->flags |= AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_FIELD_SEP;
4253        *sa = ++s;
4254        /* recurse */ view japanese
4255        return parse_errorlog_item(p, it, sa);
4256    }
4257    else if (*s == '%') {
4258        it->arg = "%";
4259        it->func = log_constant_item;
4260        *sa = ++s;
4261        return NULL;
4262    }
4263
4264    while (*s) {
4265        switch (*s) {
4266        case '{':
4267            ++s;
4268            it->arg = ap_getword(p, &s, '}');
4269            break;
4270        case '+':
4271            ++s;
4272            it->flags |= AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_REQUIRED;
4273            break;
4274        case '-':
4275            ++s;
4276            it->flags |= AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_NULL_AS_HYPHEN;
4277            break;
4278        case '0':
4279        case '1':
4280        case '2':
4281        case '3':
4282        case '4':
4283        case '5':
4284        case '6':
4285        case '7':
4286        case '8':
4287        case '9':
4288            i = *s - '0';
4289            while (apr_isdigit(*++s))
4290                i = i * 10 + (*s) - '0';
4291            it->min_loglevel = i;
4292            break;
4293        case 'M':
4294            it->func = NULL;
4295            it->flags |= AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_MESSAGE;
4296            *sa = ++s;
4297            return NULL;
4298        default:
4299            handler = (ap_errorlog_handler *)apr_hash_get(errorlog_hash, s, 1);
4300            if (!handler) {
4301                char dummy[2];
4302
4303                dummy[0] = *s;
4304                dummy[1] = '\0';
4305                return apr_pstrcat(p, "Unrecognized error log format directive %",
4306                               dummy, NULL);
4307            }
4308            it->func = handler->func;
4309            *sa = ++s;
4310            return NULL;
4311        }
4312    }
4313
4314    return "Ran off end of error log format parsing args to some directive";
4315}
4316
4317static apr_array_header_t *parse_errorlog_string(apr_pool_t *p,
4318                                                 const char *s,
4319                                                 const char **err,
4320                                                 int is_main_fmt)
4321{
4322    apr_array_header_t *a = apr_array_make(p, 30,
4323                                           sizeof(ap_errorlog_format_item));
4324    char *res;
4325    int seen_msg_fmt = 0;
4326
4327    while (s && *s) {
4328        ap_errorlog_format_item *item =
4329            (ap_errorlog_format_item *)apr_array_push(a);
4330        memset(item, 0, sizeof(*item));
4331        res = parse_errorlog_item(p, item, &s);
4332        if (res) {
4333            *err = res;
4334            return NULL;
4335        }
4336        if (item->flags & AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_MESSAGE) {
4337            if (!is_main_fmt) {
4338                *err = "%M cannot be used in once-per-request or "
4339                       "once-per-connection formats";
4340                return NULL;
4341            }
4342            seen_msg_fmt = 1;
4343        }
4344        if (is_main_fmt && item->flags & AP_ERRORLOG_FLAG_REQUIRED) {
4345            *err = "The '+' flag cannot be used in the main error log format";
4346            return NULL;
4347        }
4348        if (!is_main_fmt && item->min_loglevel) {
4349            *err = "The loglevel cannot be used as a condition in "
4350                   "once-per-request or once-per-connection formats";
4351            return NULL;
4352        }
4353        if (item->min_loglevel > APLOG_TRACE8) {
4354            *err = "The specified loglevel modifier is out of range";
4355            return NULL;
4356        }
4357    }
4358
4359    if (is_main_fmt && !seen_msg_fmt) {
4360        *err = "main ErrorLogFormat must contain message format string '%M'";
4361        return NULL;
4362    }
4363
4364    return a;
4365}
4366
4367static const char *set_errorlog_format(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy,
4368                                       const char *arg1, const char *arg2)
4369{
4370    const char *err_string = NULL;
4371    core_server_config *conf =
4372        ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
4373
4374    if (!arg2) {
4375        conf->error_log_format = parse_errorlog_string(cmd->pool, arg1,
4376                                                       &err_string, 1);
4377    }
4378    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg1, "connection")) {
4379        if (!conf->error_log_conn) {
4380            conf->error_log_conn = apr_array_make(cmd->pool, 5,
4381                                                  sizeof(apr_array_header_t *));
4382        }
4383
4384        if (*arg2) {
4385            apr_array_header_t **e;
4386            e = (apr_array_header_t **) apr_array_push(conf->error_log_conn);
4387            *e = parse_errorlog_string(cmd->pool, arg2, &err_string, 0);
4388        }
4389    }
4390    else if (!ap_cstr_casecmp(arg1, "request")) {
4391        if (!conf->error_log_req) {
4392            conf->error_log_req = apr_array_make(cmd->pool, 5,
4393                                                 sizeof(apr_array_header_t *));
4394        }
4395
4396        if (*arg2) {
4397            apr_array_header_t **e;
4398            e = (apr_array_header_t **) apr_array_push(conf->error_log_req);
4399            *e = parse_errorlog_string(cmd->pool, arg2, &err_string, 0);
4400        }
4401    }
4402    else {
4403        err_string = "ErrorLogFormat type must be one of request, connection";
4404    }
4405
4406    return err_string;
4407}
4408
4409AP_DECLARE(void) ap_register_errorlog_handler(apr_pool_t *p, char *tag,
4410                                              ap_errorlog_handler_fn_t *handler,
4411                                              int flags)
4412{
4413    ap_errorlog_handler *log_struct = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(*log_struct));
4414    log_struct->func = handler;
4415    log_struct->flags = flags;
4416
4417    apr_hash_set(errorlog_hash, tag, 1, (const void *)log_struct);
4418}
4419
4420
4421static const char *set_merge_trailers(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy, int arg)
4422{
4423    core_server_config *conf = ap_get_module_config(cmd->server->module_config,
4424                                                    &core_module);
4425    conf->merge_trailers = (arg ? AP_MERGE_TRAILERS_ENABLE :
4426            AP_MERGE_TRAILERS_DISABLE);
4427
4428    return NULL;
4429}
4430
4431#ifdef WIN32
4432static const char *set_unc_list(cmd_parms *cmd, void *d_, int argc, char *const argv[])
4433{
4434    core_server_config *sconf = ap_get_core_module_config(cmd->server->module_config);
4435    int i;
4436    const char *err;
4437
4438    if ((err = ap_check_cmd_context(cmd, NOT_IN_VIRTUALHOST)) != NULL)
4439        return err;
4440
4441    sconf->unc_list = apr_array_make(cmd->pool, argc, sizeof(char *));
4442
4443    for (i = 0; i < argc; i++) {
4444        *(char **)apr_array_push(sconf->unc_list) = apr_pstrdup(cmd->pool, argv[i]);
4445    }
4446
4447    return NULL;
4448}
4449#endif
4450/* Note --- ErrorDocument will now work from .htaccess files.
4451 * The AllowOverride of Fileinfo allows webmasters to turn it off
4452 */ view japanese
4453
4454static const command_rec core_cmds[] = {
4455
4456/* Old access config file commands */ view japanese
4457
4458AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<Directory", dirsection, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4459  "Container for directives affecting resources located in the specified "
4460  "directories"),
4461AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<Location", urlsection, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4462  "Container for directives affecting resources accessed through the "
4463  "specified URL paths"),
4464AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<VirtualHost", virtualhost_section, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4465  "Container to map directives to a particular virtual host, takes one or "
4466  "more host addresses"),
4467AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<Files", filesection, NULL, OR_ALL,
4468  "Container for directives affecting files matching specified patterns"),
4469AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<Limit", ap_limit_section, NULL, OR_LIMIT | OR_AUTHCFG,
4470  "Container for authentication directives when accessed using specified HTTP "
4471  "methods"),
4472AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<LimitExcept", ap_limit_section, (void*)1,
4473                 OR_LIMIT | OR_AUTHCFG,
4474  "Container for authentication directives to be applied when any HTTP "
4475  "method other than those specified is used to access the resource"),
4476AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<IfModule", start_cond_section, (void *)test_ifmod_section,
4477              EXEC_ON_READ | OR_ALL,
4478  "Container for directives based on existence of specified modules"),
4479AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<IfDefine", start_cond_section, (void *)test_ifdefine_section,
4480              EXEC_ON_READ | OR_ALL,
4481  "Container for directives based on existence of command line defines"),
4482AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<IfFile", start_cond_section, (void *)test_iffile_section,
4483              EXEC_ON_READ | OR_ALL,
4484  "Container for directives based on existence of files on disk"),
4485AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<IfDirective", start_cond_section, (void *)test_ifdirective_section,
4486              EXEC_ON_READ | OR_ALL,
4487  "Container for directives based on existence of named directive"),
4488AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<IfSection", start_cond_section, (void *)test_ifsection_section,
4489              EXEC_ON_READ | OR_ALL,
4490  "Container for directives based on existence of named section"),
4491AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<DirectoryMatch", dirsection, (void*)1, RSRC_CONF,
4492  "Container for directives affecting resources located in the "
4493  "specified directories"),
4494AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<LocationMatch", urlsection, (void*)1, RSRC_CONF,
4495  "Container for directives affecting resources accessed through the "
4496  "specified URL paths"),
4497AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<FilesMatch", filesection, (void*)1, OR_ALL,
4498  "Container for directives affecting files matching specified patterns"),
4499#ifdef GPROF
4500AP_INIT_TAKE1("GprofDir", set_gprof_dir, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4501  "Directory to plop gmon.out files"),
4502#endif
4503AP_INIT_TAKE1("AddDefaultCharset", set_add_default_charset, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4504  "The name of the default charset to add to any Content-Type without one or 'Off' to disable"),
4505AP_INIT_TAKE1("AcceptPathInfo", set_accept_path_info, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4506  "Set to on or off for PATH_INFO to be accepted by handlers, or default for the per-handler preference"),
4507AP_INIT_TAKE12("Define", set_define, NULL, EXEC_ON_READ|ACCESS_CONF|RSRC_CONF,
4508              "Define a variable, optionally to a value.  Same as passing -D to the command line."),
4509AP_INIT_TAKE1("UnDefine", unset_define, NULL, EXEC_ON_READ|ACCESS_CONF|RSRC_CONF,
4510              "Undefine the existence of a variable. Undo a Define."),
4511AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("Error", generate_error, NULL, OR_ALL,
4512                 "Generate error message from within configuration"),
4513AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<If", ifsection, COND_IF, OR_ALL,
4514  "Container for directives to be conditionally applied"),
4515AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<ElseIf", ifsection, COND_ELSEIF, OR_ALL,
4516  "Container for directives to be conditionally applied"),
4517AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("<Else", ifsection, COND_ELSE, OR_ALL,
4518  "Container for directives to be conditionally applied"),
4519
4520/* Old resource config file commands */ view japanese
4521
4522AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("AccessFileName", set_access_name, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4523  "Name(s) of per-directory config files (default: .htaccess)"),
4524AP_INIT_TAKE1("DocumentRoot", set_document_root, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4525  "Root directory of the document tree"),
4526AP_INIT_TAKE2("ErrorDocument", set_error_document, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4527  "Change responses for HTTP errors"),
4528AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("AllowOverride", set_override, NULL, ACCESS_CONF,
4529  "Controls what groups of directives can be configured by per-directory "
4530  "config files"),
4531AP_INIT_TAKE_ARGV("AllowOverrideList", set_override_list, NULL, ACCESS_CONF,
4532  "Controls what individual directives can be configured by per-directory "
4533  "config files"),
