4534AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("Options", set_options, NULL, OR_OPTIONS,
4535  "Set a number of attributes for a given directory"),
4536AP_INIT_TAKE1("DefaultType", set_default_type, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4537  "the default media type for otherwise untyped files (DEPRECATED)"),
4538AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("FileETag", set_etag_bits, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4539  "Specify components used to construct a file's ETag"),
4540AP_INIT_TAKE1("EnableMMAP", set_enable_mmap, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4541  "Controls whether memory-mapping may be used to read files"),
4542AP_INIT_TAKE1("EnableSendfile", set_enable_sendfile, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4543  "Controls whether sendfile may be used to transmit files"),
4544AP_INIT_TAKE1("ReadBufferSize", set_read_buf_size, NULL, ACCESS_CONF|RSRC_CONF,
4545  "Size (in bytes) of the memory buffers used to read data"),
4546AP_INIT_TAKE1("FlushMaxThreshold", set_flush_max_threshold, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4547  "Maximum threshold above which pending data are flushed to the network"),

4549  "Maximum number of pipelined responses (pending) above which they are "
4550  "flushed to the network"),
4551#ifdef WIN32
4552AP_INIT_TAKE_ARGV("UNCList", set_unc_list, NULL, RSRC_CONF|EXEC_ON_READ,
4553  "Controls what UNC hosts may be looked up"),
4554#endif
4555
4556/* Old server config file commands */ view japanese
4557
4558AP_INIT_TAKE1("Protocol", set_protocol, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4559  "Set the Protocol for httpd to use."),
4560AP_INIT_TAKE2("AcceptFilter", set_accf_map, NULL, RSRC_CONF,

4562AP_INIT_TAKE1("Port", ap_set_deprecated, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4563  "Port was replaced with Listen in Apache 2.0"),
4564AP_INIT_TAKE1("HostnameLookups", set_hostname_lookups, NULL,
4565  ACCESS_CONF|RSRC_CONF,

4573AP_INIT_TAKE1("ServerSignature", set_signature_flag, NULL, OR_ALL,

4629                 "mutex (or \"default\") and mechanism"),
4630
4631AP_INIT_TAKE1("MaxRanges", set_max_ranges, NULL, RSRC_CONF|ACCESS_CONF,
4632              "Maximum number of Ranges in a request before returning the entire "

4634AP_INIT_TAKE1("MaxRangeOverlaps", set_max_overlaps, NULL, RSRC_CONF|ACCESS_CONF,

4637AP_INIT_TAKE1("MaxRangeReversals", set_max_reversals, NULL, RSRC_CONF|ACCESS_CONF,
4638              "Maximum number of reversals in Ranges in a request before returning the entire "
4639              "resource, or 0 for unlimited"),
4640/* System Resource Controls */ view japanese

4666AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("RegexDefaultOptions", set_regex_default_options, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4667                 "default options for regexes (prefixed by '+' to add, '-' to del)"),
4668

4671              "maximum recursion depth of internal redirects and subrequests"),

4674             "Controls whether HTTP authorization headers, normally hidden, will "

4679             "Controls whether the REDIRECT_URL environment variable is fully "
4680             "qualified"),
4681AP_INIT_FLAG("StrictHostCheck", set_core_server_flag, 
4682             (void *)APR_OFFSETOF(core_server_config, strict_host_check),  
4683             RSRC_CONF,
4684             "Controls whether a hostname match is required"),
4685AP_INIT_TAKE1("ForceType", ap_set_string_slot_lower,
4686       (void *)APR_OFFSETOF(core_dir_config, mime_type), OR_FILEINFO,
4687     "a mime type that overrides other configured type"),
4688AP_INIT_TAKE1("SetHandler", set_sethandler, NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
4689   "a handler name that overrides any other configured handler"),
4690AP_INIT_TAKE1("SetOutputFilter", ap_set_string_slot,
4691       (void *)APR_OFFSETOF(core_dir_config, output_filters), OR_FILEINFO,
4692   "filter (or ; delimited list of filters) to be run on the request content"),
4693AP_INIT_TAKE1("SetInputFilter", ap_set_string_slot,
4694       (void *)APR_OFFSETOF(core_dir_config, input_filters), OR_FILEINFO,
4695   "filter (or ; delimited list of filters) to be run on the request body"),
4696AP_INIT_TAKE1("AllowEncodedSlashes", set_allow2f, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4697             "Allow URLs containing '/' encoded as '%2F'"),
4698
4699/* scoreboard.c directives */ view japanese
4700AP_INIT_TAKE1("ScoreBoardFile", ap_set_scoreboard, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4701              "A file for Apache to maintain runtime process management information"),
4702AP_INIT_FLAG("ExtendedStatus", ap_set_extended_status, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4703             "\"On\" to track extended status information, \"Off\" to disable"),
4704AP_INIT_FLAG("SeeRequestTail", ap_set_reqtail, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4705             "For extended status, \"On\" to see the last 63 chars of "
4706             "the request line, \"Off\" (default) to see the first 63"),
4707
4708/*
4709 * These are default configuration directives that mpms can/should
4710 * pay attention to.
4711 * XXX These are not for all platforms, and even some Unix MPMs might not want
4712 * some directives.
4713 */ view japanese
4714AP_INIT_TAKE1("PidFile",  ap_mpm_set_pidfile, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4715              "A file for logging the server process ID"),
4716AP_INIT_TAKE1("MaxRequestsPerChild", ap_mpm_set_max_requests, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4717              "Maximum number of connections a particular child serves before "
4718              "dying. (DEPRECATED, use MaxConnectionsPerChild)"),
4719AP_INIT_TAKE1("MaxConnectionsPerChild", ap_mpm_set_max_requests, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4720              "Maximum number of connections a particular child serves before dying."),
4721AP_INIT_TAKE1("CoreDumpDirectory", ap_mpm_set_coredumpdir, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4722              "The location of the directory Apache changes to before dumping core"),
4723AP_INIT_TAKE1("MaxMemFree", ap_mpm_set_max_mem_free, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4724              "Maximum number of 1k blocks a particular child's allocator may hold."),
4725AP_INIT_TAKE1("ThreadStackSize", ap_mpm_set_thread_stacksize, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4726              "Size in bytes of stack used by threads handling client connections"),
4727#if AP_ENABLE_EXCEPTION_HOOK
4728AP_INIT_TAKE1("EnableExceptionHook", ap_mpm_set_exception_hook, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4729              "Controls whether exception hook may be called after a crash"),
4730#endif
4731AP_INIT_TAKE1("TraceEnable", set_trace_enable, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4732              "'on' (default), 'off' or 'extended' to trace request body content"),
4733AP_INIT_FLAG("MergeTrailers", set_merge_trailers, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4734              "merge request trailers into request headers or not"),
4735AP_INIT_ITERATE("Protocols", set_protocols, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4736                "Controls which protocols are allowed"),
4737AP_INIT_TAKE1("ProtocolsHonorOrder", set_protocols_honor_order, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4738              "'off' (default) or 'on' to respect given order of protocols, "
4739              "by default the client specified order determines selection"),
4740AP_INIT_ITERATE("HttpProtocolOptions", set_http_protocol_options, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4741                "'Allow0.9' or 'Require1.0' (default); "
4742                "'RegisteredMethods' or 'LenientMethods' (default); "
4743                "'Unsafe' or 'Strict' (default). Sets HTTP acceptance rules"),
4744AP_INIT_ITERATE("RegisterHttpMethod", set_http_method, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
4745                "Registers non-standard HTTP methods"),
4746AP_INIT_FLAG("MergeSlashes", set_core_server_flag, 
4747             (void *)APR_OFFSETOF(core_server_config, merge_slashes),  
4748             RSRC_CONF,
4749             "Controls whether consecutive slashes in the URI path are merged"),
4750{ NULL }
4751};
4752
4753/*****************************************************************
4754 *
4755 * Core handlers for various phases of server operation...
4756 */ view japanese
4757
4758AP_DECLARE_NONSTD(int) ap_core_translate(request_rec *r)
4759{
4760    apr_status_t rv;
4761    char *path;
4762
4763    /* XXX this seems too specific, this should probably become
4764     * some general-case test
4765     */ view japanese
4766    if (r->proxyreq) {
4767        return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
4768    }
4769    if (!r->uri || ((r->uri[0] != '/') && strcmp(r->uri, "*"))) {
4770        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00126)
4771                     "Invalid URI in request '%s' '%s'", r->uri, r->the_request);
4772        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
4773    }
4774
4775    if (r->server->path
4776        && !strncmp(r->uri, r->server->path, r->server->pathlen)
4777        && (r->server->path[r->server->pathlen - 1] == '/'
4778            || r->uri[r->server->pathlen] == '/'
4779            || r->uri[r->server->pathlen] == '\0'))
4780    {
4781        path = r->uri + r->server->pathlen;
4782    }
4783    else {
4784        path = r->uri;
4785    }
4786    /*
4787     * Make sure that we do not mess up the translation by adding two
4788     * /'s in a row.  This happens under windows when the document
4789     * root ends with a /
4790     */ view japanese
4791    /* skip all leading /'s (e.g. http://localhost///foo)
4792     * so we are looking at only the relative path.
4793     */ view japanese
4794    while (*path == '/') {
4795        ++path;
4796    }
4797    if ((rv = apr_filepath_merge(&r->filename, ap_document_root(r), path,
4798                                 APR_FILEPATH_TRUENAME
4799                               | APR_FILEPATH_SECUREROOT, r->pool))
4800                != APR_SUCCESS) {
4801        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00127)
4802                     "Cannot map %s to file", r->the_request);
4803        return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
4804    }
4805    r->canonical_filename = r->filename;
4806
4807    return OK;
4808}
4809
4810/*****************************************************************
4811 *
4812 * Test the filesystem name through directory_walk and file_walk
4813 */ view japanese
4814static int core_map_to_storage(request_rec *r)
4815{
4816    int access_status;
4817
4818    if ((access_status = ap_directory_walk(r))) {
4819        return access_status;
4820    }
4821
4822    if ((access_status = ap_file_walk(r))) {
4823        return access_status;
4824    }
4825
4826    return OK;
4827}
4828
4829
4830static int do_nothing(request_rec *r) { return OK; }
4831
4832static int core_override_type(request_rec *r)
4833{
4834    core_dir_config *conf =
4835        (core_dir_config *)ap_get_core_module_config(r->per_dir_config);
4836
4837    /* Check for overrides with ForceType / SetHandler
4838     */ view japanese
4839    if (conf->mime_type && strcmp(conf->mime_type, "none"))
4840        ap_set_content_type_ex(r, (char*) conf->mime_type, 1);
4841
4842    if (conf->expr_handler) { 
4843        const char *err;
4844        const char *val;
4845        val = ap_expr_str_exec(r, conf->expr_handler, &err);
4846        if (err) {
4847            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(03154)
4848                          "Can't evaluate handler expression: %s", err);
4849            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4850        }
4851
4852        if (val != ap_strstr_c(val, "proxy:unix")) { 
4853            /* Retained for compatibility --  but not for UDS */ view japanese
4854            char *tmp = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, val);
4855            ap_str_tolower(tmp);
4856            val = tmp;
4857        }
4858
4859        if (strcmp(val, "none")) { 
4860            r->handler = val;
4861        }
4862    }
4863    else if (conf->handler && strcmp(conf->handler, "none")) { 
4864        r->handler = conf->handler;
4865    }
4866
4867    /* Deal with the poor soul who is trying to force path_info to be
4868     * accepted within the core_handler, where they will let the subreq
4869     * address its contents.  This is toggled by the user in the very
4870     * beginning of the fixup phase (here!), so modules should override the user's
4871     * discretion in their own module fixup phase.  It is tristate, if
4872     * the user doesn't specify, the result is AP_REQ_DEFAULT_PATH_INFO.
4873     * (which the module may interpret to its own customary behavior.)
4874     * It won't be touched if the value is no longer AP_ACCEPT_PATHINFO_UNSET,
4875     * so any module changing the value prior to the fixup phase
4876     * OVERRIDES the user's choice.
4877     */ view japanese
4878    if ((r->used_path_info == AP_REQ_DEFAULT_PATH_INFO)
4879        && (conf->accept_path_info != AP_ACCEPT_PATHINFO_UNSET)) {
4880        /* No module knew better, and the user coded AcceptPathInfo */ view japanese
4881        r->used_path_info = conf->accept_path_info;
4882    }
4883
4884    return OK;
4885}
4886
4887static int default_handler(request_rec *r)
4888{
4889    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
4890    apr_bucket_brigade *bb;
4891    apr_bucket *e;
4892    core_dir_config *d;
4893    int errstatus;
4894    apr_file_t *fd = NULL;
4895    apr_status_t status;
4896    /* XXX if/when somebody writes a content-md5 filter we either need to
4897     *     remove this support or coordinate when to use the filter vs.
4898     *     when to use this code
4899     *     The current choice of when to compute the md5 here matches the 1.3
4900     *     support fairly closely (unlike 1.3, we don't handle computing md5
4901     *     when the charset is translated).
4902     */ view japanese
4903    int bld_content_md5;
4904
4905    d = (core_dir_config *)ap_get_core_module_config(r->per_dir_config);
4906    bld_content_md5 = (d->content_md5 == AP_CONTENT_MD5_ON)
4907                      && r->output_filters->frec->ftype != AP_FTYPE_RESOURCE;
4908
4909    ap_allow_standard_methods(r, MERGE_ALLOW, M_GET, M_OPTIONS, M_POST, -1);
4910
4911    /* If filters intend to consume the request body, they must
4912     * register an InputFilter to slurp the contents of the POST
4913     * data from the POST input stream.  It no longer exists when
4914     * the output filters are invoked by the default handler.
4915     */ view japanese
4916    if ((errstatus = ap_discard_request_body(r)) != OK) {
4917        return errstatus;
4918    }
4919
4920    if (r->method_number == M_GET || r->method_number == M_POST) {
4921        if (r->finfo.filetype == APR_NOFILE) {
4922            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, r, APLOGNO(00128)
4923                          "File does not exist: %s",
4924                          apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename, r->path_info, NULL));
4925            return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
4926        }
4927
4928        /* Don't try to serve a dir.  Some OSs do weird things with
4929         * raw I/O on a dir.
4930         */ view japanese
4931        if (r->finfo.filetype == APR_DIR) {
4932            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, r, APLOGNO(00129)
4933                          "Attempt to serve directory: %s", r->filename);
4934            return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
4935        }
4936
4937        if ((r->used_path_info != AP_REQ_ACCEPT_PATH_INFO) &&
4938            r->path_info && *r->path_info)
4939        {
4940            /* default to reject */ view japanese
4941            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, r, APLOGNO(00130)
4942                          "File does not exist: %s",
4943                          apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename, r->path_info, NULL));
4944            return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
4945        }
4946
4947        /* We understood the (non-GET) method, but it might not be legal for
4948           this particular resource. Check to see if the 'deliver_script'
4949           flag is set. If so, then we go ahead and deliver the file since
4950           it isn't really content (only GET normally returns content).
4951
4952           Note: based on logic further above, the only possible non-GET
4953           method at this point is POST. In the future, we should enable
4954           script delivery for all methods.  */ view japanese
4955        if (r->method_number != M_GET) {
4956            core_request_config *req_cfg;
4957
4958            req_cfg = ap_get_core_module_config(r->request_config);
4959            if (!req_cfg->deliver_script) {
4960                /* The flag hasn't been set for this request. Punt. */ view japanese
4961                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00131)
4962                              "This resource does not accept the %s method.",
4963                              r->method);
4964                return HTTP_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED;
4965            }
4966        }
4967
4968
4969        if ((status = apr_file_open(&fd, r->filename, APR_READ | APR_BINARY
4970#if APR_HAS_SENDFILE
4971                            | AP_SENDFILE_ENABLED(d->enable_sendfile)
4972#endif
4973                                    , 0, r->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
4974            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(00132)
4975                          "file permissions deny server access: %s", r->filename);
4976            return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
4977        }
4978
4979        ap_update_mtime(r, r->finfo.mtime);
4980        ap_set_last_modified(r);
4981        ap_set_etag_fd(r, fd);
4982        ap_set_accept_ranges(r);
4983        ap_set_content_length(r, r->finfo.size);
4984        if (bld_content_md5) {
4985            apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Content-MD5",
4986                           ap_md5digest(r->pool, fd));
4987        }
4988
4989        bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool, c->bucket_alloc);
4990
4991        if ((errstatus = ap_meets_conditions(r)) != OK) {
4992            apr_file_close(fd);
4993            r->status = errstatus;
4994        }
4995        else {
4996            e = apr_brigade_insert_file(bb, fd, 0, r->finfo.size, r->pool);
4997
4998#if APR_HAS_MMAP
4999            if (d->enable_mmap == ENABLE_MMAP_OFF) {
5000                (void)apr_bucket_file_enable_mmap(e, 0);
5001            }
5002#endif
5003#if APR_MAJOR_VERSION > 1 || (APU_MAJOR_VERSION == 1 && APU_MINOR_VERSION >= 6)
5004            if (d->read_buf_size) {
5005                apr_bucket_file_set_buf_size(e, d->read_buf_size);
5006            }
5007#endif
5008        }
5009
5010        e = apr_bucket_eos_create(c->bucket_alloc);
5011        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, e);
5012
5013        status = ap_pass_brigade(r->output_filters, bb);
5014        apr_brigade_cleanup(bb);
5015
5016        if (status == APR_SUCCESS
5017            || r->status != HTTP_OK
5018            || c->aborted) {
5019            return OK;
5020        }
5021        else {
5022            /* no way to know what type of error occurred */ view japanese
5023            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, status, r, APLOGNO(00133)
5024                          "default_handler: ap_pass_brigade returned %i",
5025                          status);
5026            return AP_FILTER_ERROR;
5027        }
5028    }
5029    else {              /* unusual method (not GET or POST) */ view japanese
5030        if (r->method_number == M_INVALID) {
5031            /* See if this looks like an undecrypted SSL handshake attempt.
5032             * It's safe to look a couple bytes into the_request if it exists, as it's
5033             * always allocated at least MIN_LINE_ALLOC (80) bytes.
5034             */ view japanese
5035            if (r->the_request
5036                && r->the_request[0] == 0x16
5037                && (r->the_request[1] == 0x2 || r->the_request[1] == 0x3)) {
5038                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00134)
5039                              "Invalid method in request %s - possible attempt to establish SSL connection on non-SSL port", r->the_request);
5040            } else {
5041                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00135)
5042                              "Invalid method in request %s", r->the_request);
5043            }
5044            return HTTP_NOT_IMPLEMENTED;
5045        }
5046
5047        if (r->method_number == M_OPTIONS) {
5048            return ap_send_http_options(r);
5049        }
5050        return HTTP_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED;
5051    }
5052}
5053
5054/* Optional function coming from mod_logio, used for logging of output
5055 * traffic
5056 */ view japanese
5057APR_OPTIONAL_FN_TYPE(ap_logio_add_bytes_out) *ap__logio_add_bytes_out;
5058APR_OPTIONAL_FN_TYPE(authz_some_auth_required) *ap__authz_ap_some_auth_required;
5059
5060/* Insist that at least one module will undertake to provide system
5061 * security by dropping startup privileges.
5062 */ view japanese
5063static int sys_privileges = 0;
5064AP_DECLARE(int) ap_sys_privileges_handlers(int inc)
5065{
5066    sys_privileges += inc;
5067    return sys_privileges;
5068}
5069
5070static int check_errorlog_dir(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
5071{
5072    if (!s->error_fname || s->error_fname[0] == '|'
5073        || strcmp(s->error_fname, "syslog") == 0
5074        || strncmp(s->error_fname, "syslog:", 7) == 0) {
5075        return APR_SUCCESS;
5076    }
5077    else {
5078        char *abs = ap_server_root_relative(p, s->error_fname);
5079        char *dir = ap_make_dirstr_parent(p, abs);
5080        apr_finfo_t finfo;
5081        apr_status_t rv = apr_stat(&finfo, dir, APR_FINFO_TYPE, p);
5082        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS && finfo.filetype != APR_DIR)
5083            rv = APR_ENOTDIR;
5084        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5085            const char *desc = "main error log";
5086            if (s->defn_name)
5087                desc = apr_psprintf(p, "error log of vhost defined at %s:%d",
5088                                    s->defn_name, s->defn_line_number);
5089            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP|APLOG_EMERG, rv,
5090                          ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02291)
5091                         "Cannot access directory '%s' for %s", dir, desc);
5092            return !OK;
5093        }
5094    }
5095    return OK;
5096}
5097
5098static int core_check_config(apr_pool_t *pconf, apr_pool_t *plog, apr_pool_t *ptemp, server_rec *s)
5099{
5100    int rv = OK;
5101    while (s) {
5102        if (check_errorlog_dir(ptemp, s) != OK)
5103            rv = !OK;
5104        s = s->next;
5105    }
5106    return rv;
5107}
5108
5109
5110static int core_pre_config(apr_pool_t *pconf, apr_pool_t *plog, apr_pool_t *ptemp)
5111{
5112    ap_mutex_init(pconf);
5113
5114    if (!saved_server_config_defines)
5115        init_config_defines(pconf);
5116    apr_pool_cleanup_register(pconf, NULL, reset_config_defines,
5117                              apr_pool_cleanup_null);
5118
5119    ap_regcomp_set_default_cflags(AP_REG_DEFAULT);
5120
5121    mpm_common_pre_config(pconf);
5122
5123    return OK;
5124}
5125
5126static int core_post_config(apr_pool_t *pconf, apr_pool_t *plog, apr_pool_t *ptemp, server_rec *s)
5127{
5128    ap__logio_add_bytes_out = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_logio_add_bytes_out);
5129    ident_lookup = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_ident_lookup);
5130    ap__authz_ap_some_auth_required = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(authz_some_auth_required);
5131    authn_ap_auth_type = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(authn_ap_auth_type);
5132    authn_ap_auth_name = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(authn_ap_auth_name);
5133    access_compat_ap_satisfies = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(access_compat_ap_satisfies);
5134
5135    set_banner(pconf);
5136    ap_setup_make_content_type(pconf);
5137    ap_setup_auth_internal(ptemp);
5138    ap_setup_ssl_optional_fns(pconf);
5139    if (!sys_privileges) {
5140        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00136)
5141                     "Server MUST relinquish startup privileges before "
5142                     "accepting connections.  Please ensure mod_unixd "
5143                     "or other system security module is loaded.");
5144        return !OK;
5145    }
5146    apr_pool_cleanup_register(pconf, NULL, ap_mpm_end_gen_helper,
5147                              apr_pool_cleanup_null);
5148    return OK;
5149}
5150
5151static void core_insert_filter(request_rec *r)
5152{
5153    core_dir_config *conf = (core_dir_config *)
5154                            ap_get_core_module_config(r->per_dir_config);
5155    const char *filter, *filters = conf->output_filters;
5156
5157    if (filters) {
5158        while (*filters && (filter = ap_getword(r->pool, &filters, ';'))) {
5159            ap_add_output_filter(filter, NULL, r, r->connection);
5160        }
5161    }
5162
5163    filters = conf->input_filters;
5164    if (filters) {
5165        while (*filters && (filter = ap_getword(r->pool, &filters, ';'))) {
5166            ap_add_input_filter(filter, NULL, r, r->connection);
5167        }
5168    }
5169}
5170
5171static apr_size_t num_request_notes = AP_NUM_STD_NOTES;
5172
5173static apr_status_t reset_request_notes(void *dummy)
5174{
5175    num_request_notes = AP_NUM_STD_NOTES;
5176    return APR_SUCCESS;
5177}
5178
5179AP_DECLARE(apr_size_t) ap_register_request_note(void)
5180{
5181    apr_pool_cleanup_register(apr_hook_global_pool, NULL, reset_request_notes,
5182                              apr_pool_cleanup_null);
5183    return num_request_notes++;
5184}
5185
5186AP_DECLARE(void **) ap_get_request_note(request_rec *r, apr_size_t note_num)
5187{
5188    core_request_config *req_cfg;
5189
5190    if (note_num >= num_request_notes) {
5191        return NULL;
5192    }
5193
5194    req_cfg = (core_request_config *)
5195        ap_get_core_module_config(r->request_config);
5196
5197    if (!req_cfg) {
5198        return NULL;
5199    }
5200
5201    return &(req_cfg->notes[note_num]);
5202}
5203
5204AP_DECLARE(apr_socket_t *) ap_get_conn_socket(conn_rec *c)
5205{
5206    return ap_get_core_module_config(c->conn_config);
5207}
5208
5209static int core_create_req(request_rec *r)
5210{
5211    /* Alloc the config struct and the array of request notes in
5212     * a single block for efficiency
5213     */ view japanese
5214    core_request_config *req_cfg;
5215
5216    req_cfg = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(core_request_config) +
5217                          sizeof(void *) * num_request_notes);
5218    req_cfg->notes = (void **)((char *)req_cfg + sizeof(core_request_config));
5219
5220    /* ### temporarily enable script delivery as the default */ view japanese
5221    req_cfg->deliver_script = 1;
5222
5223    if (r->main) {
5224        core_request_config *main_req_cfg = (core_request_config *)
5225            ap_get_core_module_config(r->main->request_config);
5226        req_cfg->bb = main_req_cfg->bb;
5227    }
5228    else {
5229        req_cfg->bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool, r->connection->bucket_alloc);
5230    }
5231
5232    ap_set_core_module_config(r->request_config, req_cfg);
5233
5234    return OK;
5235}
5236
5237static int core_create_proxy_req(request_rec *r, request_rec *pr)
5238{
5239    return core_create_req(pr);
5240}
5241
5242static conn_rec *core_create_conn(apr_pool_t *ptrans, server_rec *server,
5243                                  apr_socket_t *csd, long id, void *sbh,
5244                                  apr_bucket_alloc_t *alloc)
5245{
5246    apr_status_t rv;
5247    conn_rec *c = (conn_rec *) apr_pcalloc(ptrans, sizeof(conn_rec));
5248
5249    c->sbh = sbh;
5250    ap_update_child_status(c->sbh, SERVER_BUSY_READ, NULL);
5251
5252    /* Got a connection structure, so initialize what fields we can
5253     * (the rest are zeroed out by pcalloc).
5254     */ view japanese
5255    c->pool = ptrans;
5256    c->conn_config = ap_create_conn_config(ptrans);
5257    c->notes = apr_table_make(ptrans, 5);
5258
5259    if ((rv = apr_socket_addr_get(&c->local_addr, APR_LOCAL, csd))
5260        != APR_SUCCESS) {
5261        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, rv, server, APLOGNO(00137)
5262                     "apr_socket_addr_get(APR_LOCAL)");
5263        apr_socket_close(csd);
5264        return NULL;
5265    }
5266    if (apr_sockaddr_ip_get(&c->local_ip, c->local_addr)) {
5267#if APR_HAVE_SOCKADDR_UN
5268        if (c->local_addr->family == APR_UNIX) {
5269            c->local_ip = apr_pstrndup(c->pool, c->local_addr->ipaddr_ptr,
5270                                       c->local_addr->ipaddr_len);
5271        }
5272        else
5273#endif
5274        c->local_ip = apr_pstrdup(c->pool, "unknown");
5275    }
5276
5277    if ((rv = apr_socket_addr_get(&c->client_addr, APR_REMOTE, csd))
5278        != APR_SUCCESS) {
5279        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, rv, server, APLOGNO(00138)
5280                     "apr_socket_addr_get(APR_REMOTE)");
5281        apr_socket_close(csd);
5282        return NULL;
5283    }
5284    if (apr_sockaddr_ip_get(&c->client_ip, c->client_addr)) {
5285#if APR_HAVE_SOCKADDR_UN
5286        if (c->client_addr->family == APR_UNIX) {
5287            c->client_ip = apr_pstrndup(c->pool, c->client_addr->ipaddr_ptr,
5288                                        c->client_addr->ipaddr_len);
5289        }
5290        else
5291#endif
5292        c->client_ip = apr_pstrdup(c->pool, "unknown");
5293    }
5294
5295    c->base_server = server;
5296
5297    c->id = id;
5298    c->bucket_alloc = alloc;
5299
5300    c->clogging_input_filters = 0;
5301
5302    return c;
5303}
5304
5305static int core_pre_connection(conn_rec *c, void *csd)
5306{
5307    core_net_rec *net;
5308    apr_status_t rv;
5309
5310    if (c->master) {
5311        return DONE;
5312    }
5313    
5314    net = apr_palloc(c->pool, sizeof(*net));
5315    /* The Nagle algorithm says that we should delay sending partial
5316     * packets in hopes of getting more data.  We don't want to do
5317     * this; we are not telnet.  There are bad interactions between
5318     * persistent connections and Nagle's algorithm that have very severe
5319     * performance penalties.  (Failing to disable Nagle is not much of a
5320     * problem with simple HTTP.)
5321     */ view japanese
5322    rv = apr_socket_opt_set(csd, APR_TCP_NODELAY, 1);
5323    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && rv != APR_ENOTIMPL) {
5324        /* expected cause is that the client disconnected already,
5325         * hence the debug level
5326         */ view japanese
5327        ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, c, APLOGNO(00139)
5328                      "apr_socket_opt_set(APR_TCP_NODELAY)");
5329    }
5330
5331    /* The core filter requires the timeout mode to be set, which
5332     * incidentally sets the socket to be nonblocking.  If this
5333     * is not initialized correctly, Linux - for example - will
5334     * be initially blocking, while Solaris will be non blocking
5335     * and any initial read will fail.
5336     */ view japanese
5337    rv = apr_socket_timeout_set(csd, c->base_server->timeout);
5338    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5339        /* expected cause is that the client disconnected already */ view japanese
5340        ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, c, APLOGNO(00140)
5341                      "apr_socket_timeout_set");
5342    }
5343
5344    net->c = c;
5345    net->in_ctx = NULL;
5346    net->out_ctx = NULL;
5347    net->client_socket = csd;
5348
5349    ap_set_core_module_config(net->c->conn_config, csd);
5350    ap_add_input_filter_handle(ap_core_input_filter_handle, net, NULL, net->c);
5351    ap_add_output_filter_handle(ap_core_output_filter_handle, net, NULL, net->c);
5352    return DONE;
5353}
5354
5355AP_DECLARE(int) ap_pre_connection(conn_rec *c, void *csd)
5356{
5357    int rc = OK;
5358
5359    rc = ap_run_pre_connection(c, csd);
5360    if (rc != OK && rc != DONE) {
5361        c->aborted = 1;
5362        /*
5363         * In case we errored, the pre_connection hook of the core
5364         * module maybe did not run (it is APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST) and
5365         * hence we missed to
5366         *
5367         * - Put the socket in c->conn_config
5368         * - Setup core output and input filters
5369         * - Set socket options and timeouts
5370         *
5371         * Hence call it in this case.
5372         */ view japanese
5373        if (!ap_get_conn_socket(c)) {
5374            core_pre_connection(c, csd);
5375        }
5376    }
5377    return rc;
5378}
5379
5380AP_DECLARE(int) ap_state_query(int query)
5381{
5382    switch (query) {
5383    case AP_SQ_MAIN_STATE:
5384        return ap_main_state;
5385    case AP_SQ_RUN_MODE:
5386        return ap_run_mode;
5387    case AP_SQ_CONFIG_GEN:
5388        return ap_config_generation;
5389    default:
5390        return AP_SQ_NOT_SUPPORTED;
5391    }
5392}
5393
5394static apr_random_t *rng = NULL;
5395#if APR_HAS_THREADS
5396static apr_thread_mutex_t *rng_mutex = NULL;
5397#endif
5398
5399static void core_child_init(apr_pool_t *pchild, server_rec *s)
5400{
5401    apr_proc_t proc;
5402#if APR_HAS_THREADS
5403    int threaded_mpm;
5404    if (ap_mpm_query(AP_MPMQ_IS_THREADED, &threaded_mpm) == APR_SUCCESS
5405        && threaded_mpm)
5406    {
5407        apr_thread_mutex_create(&rng_mutex, APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT, pchild);
5408    }
5409#endif
5410    /* The MPMs use plain fork() and not apr_proc_fork(), so we have to call
5411     * apr_random_after_fork() manually in the child
5412     */ view japanese
5413    proc.pid = getpid();
5414    apr_random_after_fork(&proc);
5415}
5416
5417static void core_optional_fn_retrieve(void)
5418{
5419    ap_init_scoreboard(NULL);
5420}
5421
5422AP_CORE_DECLARE(void) ap_random_parent_after_fork(void)
5423{
5424    /*
5425     * To ensure that the RNG state in the parent changes after the fork, we
5426     * pull some data from the RNG and discard it. This ensures that the RNG
5427     * states in the children are different even after the pid wraps around.
5428     * As we only use apr_random for insecure random bytes, pulling 2 bytes
5429     * should be enough.
5430     * XXX: APR should probably have some dedicated API to do this, but it
5431     * XXX: currently doesn't.
5432     */ view japanese
5433    apr_uint16_t data;
5434    apr_random_insecure_bytes(rng, &data, sizeof(data));
5435}
5436
5437AP_CORE_DECLARE(void) ap_init_rng(apr_pool_t *p)
5438{
5439    unsigned char seed[8];
5440    apr_status_t rv;
5441    rng = apr_random_standard_new(p);
5442    do {
5443        rv = apr_generate_random_bytes(seed, sizeof(seed));
5444        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS)
5445            goto error;
5446        apr_random_add_entropy(rng, seed, sizeof(seed));
5447        rv = apr_random_insecure_ready(rng);
5448    } while (rv == APR_ENOTENOUGHENTROPY);
5449    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS)
5450        return;
5451error:
5452    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, NULL, APLOGNO(00141)
5453                 "Could not initialize random number generator");
5454    exit(1);
5455}
5456
5457AP_DECLARE(void) ap_random_insecure_bytes(void *buf, apr_size_t size)
5458{
5459#if APR_HAS_THREADS
5460    if (rng_mutex)
5461        apr_thread_mutex_lock(rng_mutex);
5462#endif
5463    /* apr_random_insecure_bytes can only fail with APR_ENOTENOUGHENTROPY,
5464     * and we have ruled that out during initialization. Therefore we don't
5465     * need to check the return code.
5466     */ view japanese
5467    apr_random_insecure_bytes(rng, buf, size);
5468#if APR_HAS_THREADS
5469    if (rng_mutex)
5470        apr_thread_mutex_unlock(rng_mutex);
5471#endif
5472}
5473
5474/*
5475 * Finding a random number in a range.
5476 *      n' = a + n(b-a+1)/(M+1)
5477 * where:
5478 *      n' = random number in range
5479 *      a  = low end of range
5480 *      b  = high end of range
5481 *      n  = random number of 0..M
5482 *      M  = maxint
5483 * Algorithm 'borrowed' from PHP's rand() function.
5484 */ view japanese
5485#define RAND_RANGE(__n, __min, __max, __tmax) \
5486(__n) = (__min) + (long) ((double) ((__max) - (__min) + 1.0) * ((__n) / ((__tmax) + 1.0)))
5487AP_DECLARE(apr_uint32_t) ap_random_pick(apr_uint32_t min, apr_uint32_t max)
5488{
5489    apr_uint32_t number;
5490#if (!__GNUC__ || __GNUC__ >= 5 || (__GNUC__ == 4 && __GNUC_MINOR__ >= 8) || \
5491     !__sparc__ || APR_SIZEOF_VOIDP != 8)
5492    /* This triggers a gcc bug on sparc/64bit with gcc < 4.8, PR 52900 */ view japanese
5493    if (max < 16384) {
5494        apr_uint16_t num16;
5495        ap_random_insecure_bytes(&num16, sizeof(num16));
5496        RAND_RANGE(num16, min, max, APR_UINT16_MAX);
5497        number = num16;
5498    }
5499    else
5500#endif
5501    {
5502        ap_random_insecure_bytes(&number, sizeof(number));
5503        RAND_RANGE(number, min, max, APR_UINT32_MAX);
5504    }
5505    return number;
5506}
5507
5508static apr_status_t core_insert_network_bucket(conn_rec *c,
5509                                               apr_bucket_brigade *bb,
5510                                               apr_socket_t *socket)
5511{
5512    apr_bucket *e = apr_bucket_socket_create(socket, c->bucket_alloc);
5513    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, e);
5514    return APR_SUCCESS;
5515}
5516
5517static apr_status_t core_dirwalk_stat(apr_finfo_t *finfo, request_rec *r,
5518                                      apr_int32_t wanted)
5519{
5520    apr_status_t rv = ap_stat_check(r->filename, r->pool);
5521    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
5522        rv = apr_stat(finfo, r->filename, wanted, r->pool);
5523    }
5524    return rv;
5525}
5526
5527static void core_dump_config(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
5528{
5529    core_server_config *sconf = ap_get_core_module_config(s->module_config);
5530    apr_file_t *out = NULL;
5531    const char *tmp;
5532    const char **defines;
5533    int i;
5534    if (!ap_exists_config_define("DUMP_RUN_CFG"))
5535        return;
5536
5537    apr_file_open_stdout(&out, p);
5538    apr_file_printf(out, "ServerRoot: \"%s\"\n", ap_server_root);
5539    tmp = ap_server_root_relative(p, sconf->ap_document_root);
5540    apr_file_printf(out, "Main DocumentRoot: \"%s\"\n", tmp);
5541    if (s->error_fname[0] != '|'
5542        && strcmp(s->error_fname, "syslog") != 0
5543        && strncmp(s->error_fname, "syslog:", 7) != 0)
5544        tmp = ap_server_root_relative(p, s->error_fname);
5545    else
5546        tmp = s->error_fname;
5547    apr_file_printf(out, "Main ErrorLog: \"%s\"\n", tmp);
5548    if (ap_scoreboard_fname) {
5549        tmp = ap_server_root_relative(p, ap_scoreboard_fname);
5550        apr_file_printf(out, "ScoreBoardFile: \"%s\"\n", tmp);
5551    }
5552    ap_dump_mutexes(p, s, out);
5553    ap_mpm_dump_pidfile(p, out);
5554
5555    defines = (const char **)ap_server_config_defines->elts;
5556    for (i = 0; i < ap_server_config_defines->nelts; i++) {
5557        const char *name = defines[i];
5558        const char *val = NULL;
5559        if (server_config_defined_vars)
5560           val = apr_table_get(server_config_defined_vars, name);
5561        if (val)
5562            apr_file_printf(out, "Define: %s=%s\n", name, val);
5563        else
5564            apr_file_printf(out, "Define: %s\n", name);
5565    }
5566}
5567
5568static int core_upgrade_handler(request_rec *r)
5569{
5570    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
5571    const char *upgrade;
5572
5573    if (c->master) {
5574        /* Not possible to perform an HTTP/1.1 upgrade from a slave
5575         * connection. */ view japanese
5576        return DECLINED;
5577    }
5578    
5579    upgrade = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Upgrade");
5580    if (upgrade && *upgrade) {
5581        const char *conn = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Connection");
5582        if (ap_find_token(r->pool, conn, "upgrade")) {
5583            apr_array_header_t *offers = NULL;
5584            const char *err;
5585            
5586            err = ap_parse_token_list_strict(r->pool, upgrade, &offers, 0);
5587            if (err) {
5588                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02910)
5589                              "parsing Upgrade header: %s", err);
5590                return DECLINED;
5591            }
5592            
5593            if (offers && offers->nelts > 0) {
5594                const char *protocol = ap_select_protocol(c, r, NULL, offers);
5595                if (protocol && strcmp(protocol, ap_get_protocol(c))) {
5596                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02909)
5597                                  "Upgrade selects '%s'", protocol);
5598                    /* Let the client know what we are upgrading to. */ view japanese
5599                    apr_table_clear(r->headers_out);
5600                    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Upgrade", protocol);
5601                    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Connection", "Upgrade");
5602                    
5603                    r->status = HTTP_SWITCHING_PROTOCOLS;
5604                    r->status_line = ap_get_status_line(r->status);
5605                    ap_send_interim_response(r, 1);
5606
5607                    ap_switch_protocol(c, r, r->server, protocol);
5608
5609                    /* make sure httpd closes the connection after this */ view japanese
5610                    c->keepalive = AP_CONN_CLOSE;
5611                    return DONE;
5612                }
5613            }
5614        }
5615    }
5616    else if (!c->keepalives) {
5617        /* first request on a master connection, if we have protocols other
5618         * than the current one enabled here, announce them to the
5619         * client. If the client is already talking a protocol with requests
5620         * on slave connections, leave it be. */ view japanese
5621        const apr_array_header_t *upgrades;
5622        ap_get_protocol_upgrades(c, r, NULL, 0, &upgrades);
5623        if (upgrades && upgrades->nelts > 0) {
5624            char *protocols = apr_array_pstrcat(r->pool, upgrades, ',');
5625            apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Upgrade", protocols);
5626            apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Connection", "Upgrade");
5627        }
5628    }
5629    
5630    return DECLINED;
5631}
5632
5633static int core_upgrade_storage(request_rec *r)
5634{
5635    if ((r->method_number == M_OPTIONS) && r->uri && (r->uri[0] == '*') &&
5636        (r->uri[1] == '\0')) {
5637        return core_upgrade_handler(r);
5638    }
5639    return DECLINED;
5640}
5641
5642static apr_status_t core_get_pollfd_from_conn(conn_rec *c,
5643                                              struct apr_pollfd_t *pfd,
5644                                              apr_interval_time_t *ptimeout)
5645{
5646    if (c && !c->master) {
5647        pfd->desc_type = APR_POLL_SOCKET;
5648        pfd->desc.s = ap_get_conn_socket(c);
5649        if (ptimeout) {
5650            apr_socket_timeout_get(pfd->desc.s, ptimeout);
5651        }
5652        return APR_SUCCESS;
5653    }
5654    return APR_ENOTIMPL;
5655}
5656
5657AP_CORE_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_get_pollfd_from_conn(conn_rec *c,
5658                                      struct apr_pollfd_t *pfd,
5659                                      apr_interval_time_t *ptimeout)
5660{
5661    return ap_run_get_pollfd_from_conn(c, pfd, ptimeout);
5662}
5663
5664#ifdef WIN32
5665static apr_status_t check_unc(const char *path, apr_pool_t *p)
5666{
5667    int i;
5668    char *s, *teststring;
5669    apr_status_t rv = APR_EACCES;
5670    core_server_config *sconf = NULL;
5671
5672    if (!ap_server_conf) {
5673        return APR_SUCCESS; /* this early, if we have a UNC, it's specified by an admin */ view japanese
5674    }
5675
5676    if (!path || !((path[0] == '\\' || path[0] == '/')
5677                && (path[1] == '\\' || path[1] == '/'))) {
5678        return APR_SUCCESS; /* not a UNC */ view japanese
5679    }
5680
5681    sconf = ap_get_core_module_config(ap_server_conf->module_config);
5682    s = teststring = apr_pstrdup(p, path);
5683    *s++ = '/';
5684    *s++ = '/';
5685
5686    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE4, 0, ap_server_conf, 
5687                 "check_unc: check converted path %s allowed %d", 
5688                 teststring,
5689                 sconf->unc_list ? sconf->unc_list->nelts : 0);
5690
5691    for (i = 0; sconf->unc_list && i < sconf->unc_list->nelts; i++) {
5692        char *configured_unc = ((char **)sconf->unc_list->elts)[i];
5693        apr_uri_t uri;
5694        if (APR_SUCCESS == apr_uri_parse(p, teststring, &uri) &&
5695                (uri.hostinfo == NULL || 
5696                 !ap_cstr_casecmp(uri.hostinfo, configured_unc))) { 
5697            rv = APR_SUCCESS;
5698            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE4, 0, ap_server_conf, 
5699                         "check_unc: match %s %s", 
5700                         uri.hostinfo, configured_unc);
5701            break;
5702        }
5703        else { 
5704            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE4, 0, ap_server_conf, 
5705                         "check_unc: no match %s %s", uri.hostinfo, 
5706                         configured_unc);
5707        }
5708    }
5709    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5710        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(10504)
5711                     "check_unc: UNC path %s not allowed by UNCList", teststring);
5712    }
5713
5714    return rv;
5715}
5716#endif
5717
5718AP_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_filepath_merge(char **newpath,
5719                                            const char *rootpath,
5720                                            const char *addpath,
5721                                            apr_int32_t flags,
5722                                            apr_pool_t *p)
5723{
5724#ifdef WIN32
5725    apr_status_t rv;
5726
5727    if (APR_SUCCESS != (rv = check_unc(rootpath, p))) {
5728        return rv;
5729    }
5730    if (APR_SUCCESS != (rv = check_unc(addpath, p))) {
5731        return rv;
5732    }
5733#undef apr_filepath_merge
5734#endif
5735    return apr_filepath_merge(newpath, rootpath, addpath, flags, p);
5736#ifdef WIN32
5737#define apr_filepath_merge ap_filepath_merge
5738#endif
5739}
5740
5741#ifdef WIN32
5742AP_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_stat_check(const char *path, apr_pool_t *p)
5743{
5744   return check_unc(path, p);
5745}
5746#else
5747AP_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_stat_check(const char *path, apr_pool_t *p)
5748{
5749    return APR_SUCCESS;
5750}
5751#endif
5752
5753static void register_hooks(apr_pool_t *p)
5754{
5755    errorlog_hash = apr_hash_make(p);
5756    ap_register_log_hooks(p);
5757    ap_register_config_hooks(p);
5758    ap_expr_init(p);
5759
5760    /* create_connection and pre_connection should always be hooked
5761     * APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST by core to give other modules the opportunity
5762     * to install alternate network transports and stop other functions
5763     * from being run.
5764     */ view japanese
5765    ap_hook_create_connection(core_create_conn, NULL, NULL,
5766                              APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5767    ap_hook_pre_connection(core_pre_connection, NULL, NULL,
5768                           APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5769
5770    ap_hook_pre_config(core_pre_config, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5771    ap_hook_post_config(core_post_config,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5772    ap_hook_check_config(core_check_config,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_FIRST);
5773    ap_hook_test_config(core_dump_config,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_FIRST);
5774    ap_hook_translate_name(ap_core_translate,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5775    ap_hook_map_to_storage(core_upgrade_storage,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5776    ap_hook_map_to_storage(core_map_to_storage,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5777    ap_hook_open_logs(ap_open_logs,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5778    ap_hook_child_init(core_child_init,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5779    ap_hook_child_init(ap_logs_child_init,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5780    ap_hook_handler(core_upgrade_handler,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5781    ap_hook_handler(default_handler,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5782    /* FIXME: I suspect we can eliminate the need for these do_nothings - Ben */ view japanese
5783    ap_hook_type_checker(do_nothing,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5784    ap_hook_fixups(core_override_type,NULL,NULL,APR_HOOK_REALLY_FIRST);
5785    ap_hook_create_request(core_create_req, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5786    APR_OPTIONAL_HOOK(proxy, create_req, core_create_proxy_req, NULL, NULL,
5787                      APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5788    ap_hook_pre_mpm(ap_create_scoreboard, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5789    ap_hook_child_status(ap_core_child_status, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5790    ap_hook_insert_network_bucket(core_insert_network_bucket, NULL, NULL,
5791                                  APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5792    ap_hook_dirwalk_stat(core_dirwalk_stat, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5793    ap_hook_open_htaccess(ap_open_htaccess, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5794    ap_hook_optional_fn_retrieve(core_optional_fn_retrieve, NULL, NULL,
5795                                 APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5796    ap_hook_get_pollfd_from_conn(core_get_pollfd_from_conn, NULL, NULL,
5797                                 APR_HOOK_REALLY_LAST);
5798
5799    /* register the core's insert_filter hook and register core-provided
5800     * filters
5801     */ view japanese
5802    ap_hook_insert_filter(core_insert_filter, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5803
5804    ap_core_input_filter_handle =
5805        ap_register_input_filter("CORE_IN", ap_core_input_filter,
5806                                 NULL, AP_FTYPE_NETWORK);
5807    ap_content_length_filter_handle =
5808        ap_register_output_filter("CONTENT_LENGTH", ap_content_length_filter,
5809                                  NULL, AP_FTYPE_PROTOCOL);
5810    ap_core_output_filter_handle =
5811        ap_register_output_filter("CORE", ap_core_output_filter,
5812                                  NULL, AP_FTYPE_NETWORK);
5813    ap_subreq_core_filter_handle =
5814        ap_register_output_filter("SUBREQ_CORE", ap_sub_req_output_filter,
5815                                  NULL, AP_FTYPE_CONTENT_SET);
5816    ap_old_write_func =
5817        ap_register_output_filter("OLD_WRITE", ap_old_write_filter,
5818                                  NULL, AP_FTYPE_RESOURCE - 10);
5819}
5820
5821AP_DECLARE_MODULE(core) = {
5822    MPM20_MODULE_STUFF,
5823    AP_PLATFORM_REWRITE_ARGS_HOOK, /* hook to run before apache parses args */ view japanese
5824    create_core_dir_config,       /* create per-directory config structure */ view japanese
5825    merge_core_dir_configs,       /* merge per-directory config structures */ view japanese
5826    create_core_server_config,    /* create per-server config structure */ view japanese
5827    merge_core_server_configs,    /* merge per-server config structures */ view japanese
5828    core_cmds,                    /* command apr_table_t */ view japanese
5829    register_hooks                /* register hooks */ view japanese
5830};

Japanese translation

server/core.c:115-124

/* サーバーコアモジュール... このモジュールは、他のモジュールの動作を制御するオプションやコマンドを含む、非常に基本的なサーバー操作のサポートを提供します。これを官僚機構モジュールと考えてください。
 *
 * コアモジュールは、コアモジュール「のみ」を持つサーバーが実際にドキュメントを提供できるようにするための、最低限の処理を行うハンドラなども定義します。
 *
 * このファイルは、http_conf_globals に影響を与える部分を除けば、ほぼ mod_core.c と言えるものです。
 */

Original comment

/* Server core module... This module provides support for really basic
 * server operations, including options and commands which control the
 * operation of other modules.  Consider this the bureaucracy module.
 *
 * The core module also defines handlers, etc., to handle just enough
 * to allow a server with the core module ONLY to actually serve documents.
 *
 * This file could almost be mod_core.c, except for the stuff which affects
 * the http_conf_globals.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:126

/* coreのmodule_indexは0であることがわかっている */

Original comment

/* we know core's module_index is 0 */

Japanese translation

/* コアフィルター用のハンドル */

Original comment

/* Handles for core filters */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:136

/* ap_document_root_check のストレージとデフォルト値 = true を提供 */

Original comment

/* Provide ap_document_root_check storage and default value = true */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:139

/* ErrorDocument xxx "default" 用のマジックポインタ */

Original comment

/* magic pointer for ErrorDocument xxx "default" */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:142-144

/* pconf から割り当てられたグローバル状態：ここにある変数は、
 * プールがクリアされた後に変数が再利用されるのを防ぐため、
 * pconf のクリーンアップである reset_config() で必ずクリア/リセットされなければならない。 */

Original comment

/* Global state allocated out of pconf: variables here MUST be
 * cleared/reset in reset_config(), a pconf cleanup, to avoid the
 * variable getting reused after the pool is cleared. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:159

/* conf->r および conf->d[_*] は dirsection() によって初期化されるか、NULL のままとなります */

Original comment

/* conf->r and conf->d[_*] are initialized by dirsection() or left NULL */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:174-181

/*
     * NULLのままにする (apr_pcallocを使用するため):
     * conf->limit_cpu = NULL;
     * conf->limit_mem = NULL;
     * conf->limit_nproc = NULL;
     * conf->sec_file = NULL;
     * conf->sec_if   = NULL;
     */

Original comment

/*
     * left as NULL (we use apr_pcalloc):
     * conf->limit_cpu = NULL;
     * conf->limit_mem = NULL;
     * conf->limit_nproc = NULL;
     * conf->sec_file = NULL;
     * conf->sec_if   = NULL;
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:191-197

/* すべてのネゴシエーションを上書きする
     * apr_pcalloc により NULL に設定:
     * conf->mime_type = NULL;
     * conf->handler = NULL;
     * conf->output_filters = NULL;
     * conf->input_filters = NULL;
     */

Original comment

/* Overriding all negotiation
     * Set NULL by apr_pcalloc:
     * conf->mime_type = NULL;
     * conf->handler = NULL;
     * conf->output_filters = NULL;
     * conf->input_filters = NULL;
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:199-201

/*
     * ETagでinodeを使用するためのフラグ。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Flag for use of inodes in ETags.
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:227-229

/* ベースを複製し、new-> の値が存在する箇所を置換（またはマージ用にコピーを作成）して、この conf を作成する。
     */

Original comment

/* Create this conf by duplicating the base, replacing elements
     * (or creating copies for merging) where new-> values exist.
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:240-242

/* new->opts の明示的な設定がなかったため、マージを行います。
         * 不変条件 (opts_add & opts_remove) == 0 を維持します。
         */

Original comment

/* there was no explicit setting of new->opts, so we merge
         * preserve the invariant (opts_add & opts_remove) == 0
         */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:248-250

/* ベース設定で Includes が exec 付きで有効になっていたが、
         * 新しい設定で exec なしで有効になった場合は、
         * マージされたセットで exec を無効にする。 */

Original comment

/* If Includes was enabled with exec in the base config, but
         * was enabled without exec in the new config, then disable
         * exec in the merged set. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:259-261

/* それ以外の場合は単にコピーします。明示的な opts 設定は、
         * それ以前のすべての +/- 修飾子を上書きするためです。
         */

Original comment

/* otherwise we just copy, because an explicit opts setting
         * overrides all earlier +/- modifiers
         */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:286-287

/* それ以外の場合は、単に base->response_code_exprs 配列を使用します
     */

Original comment

/* Otherwise we simply use the base->response_code_exprs array
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:349-350

/* マージする場合、マージ結果は独自の配列を持つ必要がある
         */

Original comment

/* If we merge, the merge-result must have its own array
         */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:342-343

/* それ以外の場合は、単に base->sec_file 配列を使用します
     */

Original comment

/* Otherwise we simply use the base->sec_file array
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:353-354

/* それ以外の場合は、単に base->sec_if 配列を使用します
     */

Original comment

/* Otherwise we simply use the base->sec_if array

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* Overriding all negotiation

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* httpready broken before 4.1.1 */

Japanese translation

/* グローバルデフォルト / グローバルのみの設定 */

Original comment

/* global-default / global-only settings */

Japanese translation

/* マッピングはグローバルコンテキストにおいてのみ意味をなす */

Original comment

/* A mapping only makes sense in the global context */

Original comment

/* 'data' is disabled on Windows due to a DoS vuln (PR 59970) */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:498

/* vhostの読み込み中はメインのErrorLogFormatを使用する */

Original comment

/* Use main ErrorLogFormat while the vhost is loading */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:506

/* 初期化、グローバルコンテキストに対する特別なケースなし */

Original comment

/* initialization, no special case for global context */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:511-519

/* pcalloc済み - NULL/0で初期化されている
    conf->gprof_dir = NULL;

    ** 再帰ストッパー; 0 == 未設定
    conf->redirect_limit = 0;
    conf->subreq_limit = 0;

    conf->protocol = NULL;
     */

Original comment

/* pcalloc'ed - we have NULL's/0's
    conf->gprof_dir = NULL;

    ** recursion stopper; 0 == unset
    conf->redirect_limit = 0;
    conf->subreq_limit = 0;

    conf->protocol = NULL;
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:545

/* XXX virt->sec_ 配列が空の場合に base->sec_ ポインタを保持するように最適化する */

Original comment

/* XXX optimize to keep base->sec_ pointers if virt->sec_ array is empty */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:567

/* virt->accf_map に対するアクションなし、vhost ごとの設定は許可されていません */

Original comment

/* no action for virt->accf_map, not allowed per-vhost */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:612-614

/* ディレクトリごとの設定エントリを追加します（<directory>セクション用）。
 * これらはコアサーバー設定の一部です。
 */

Original comment

/* Add per-directory configuration entry (for <directory> section);
 * these are part of the core server config.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:676-680

/* 安定ソートを行う必要がありますが、qsortは安定ではありません。
 * そのため、安定させるためにリスト内の元のインデックスを保持し、
 * ソート時の第2キーとして使用します。第1キーはコンポーネント数
 * （ルートコンポーネントは0）です。
 */

Original comment

/* We need to do a stable sort, qsort isn't stable.  So to make it stable
 * we'll be maintaining the original index into the list, and using it
 * as the minor key during sorting.  The major key is the number of
 * components (where the root component is zero).
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:696-697

/* 正規表現は常に非正規表現の後にソートされる */

Original comment

/* a regex always sorts after a non-regex
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:705-706

/* 次にコンポーネント数で常にソートします
     */

Original comment

/* we always sort next by the number of components
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:714-716

/* コンポーネント数は同じであるため、設定ファイル内の元の順序を維持するために、
     * マイナーキーを比較する必要があります。
     */

Original comment

/* They have the same number of components, we now have to compare
     * the minor key to maintain the original order (from the config.)
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:736

/* すでにソートされている単純なケース... */

Original comment

/* simple case of already being sorted... */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:737-740

/* この条件をチェックしているのは高速化のためではなく、
         * ゼロバイトの palloc を試行するのを避けるためである。
         * これを行うと、一部のメモリデバッガがエラーを出す可能性があるため。
         */

Original comment

/* We're not checking this condition to be fast... we're checking
         * it to avoid trying to palloc zero bytes, which can trigger some
         * memory debuggers to barf
         */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:744

/* 安定ソートを行うために一時領域を割り当てる必要がある */

Original comment

/* we have to allocate tmp space to do a stable sort */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:755

/* そして、元の配列にコピーし直す */

Original comment

/* and now copy back to the original array */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:763-769

/*****************************************************************
 *
 * コア設定構造体の一部の要素については、他のモジュールも正当な関心を持っています
 * （これは見苦しいですが、NCSAとの後方互換性を維持するために必要です）。
 * そのため、ここに多数のアクセサを用意しています...
 */

Original comment

/*****************************************************************
 *
 * There are some elements of the core config structures in which
 * other modules have a legitimate interest (this is ugly, but necessary
 * to preserve NCSA back-compatibility).  So, we have a bunch of accessors
 * here...
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:787-790

/*
 * mod_authn_core から提供されるオプションの関数。
 * 認証のタイプを取得するために使用される。
 */

/*
 * Optional function coming from mod_authn_core, used for
 * retrieving the type of authorization
 */

Japanese translation

/*

 * 認可レルムを取得するために使用される。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Optional function coming from mod_authn_core, used for
 * retrieving the authorization realm
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:815-818

/*
 * mod_access_compat から提供されるオプションの関数。
   アクセス制御が認証/認可とどのように相互作用するかを決定するために使用される
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Optional function coming from mod_access_compat, used to determine how
   access control interacts with authentication/authorization
 */

Japanese translation

/* これを使用しないでください！ */

 */

 * part of the core anyway (and in fact, it isn't publicised to other
 * modules).
 */

server/core.c:1097-1101

/*
     * 目的の情報を返します。リモートDNS名が見つかった場合はその名前を、
     * そうでない場合は、ホスト名が要求されていたらNULLを、
     * 識別子が要求されていたらIPアドレスを返します。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Return the desired information; either the remote DNS name, if found,
     * or either NULL (if the hostname was requested) or the IP address
     * (if any identifier was requested).
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1043-1045

/* read_request フックが r->useragent_addr を設定する前に
     * ホストを調査する際は、ここでガードする
     */

Original comment

/* Guard here when examining the host before the read_request hook
     * has populated an r->useragent_addr
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1116-1118

/*
 * mod_ident から提供されるオプションの関数。ident ユーザーの検索に使用される
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Optional function coming from mod_ident, used for looking up ident user
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1134-1144

/* サーバーの「名前」をどうするかについては、2つの選択肢があります。
 * ServerName と Port によって定義される「正規（canonical）」名か、
 * Host: ヘッダーや完全な URI によって提供される「クライアントの名前」かです。
 *
 * UseCanonicalName の DNS オプションを使用すると、このルーチンは接続のローカル IP アドレスに対して逆引きを行い、
 * それを ServerName として使用します。これにより、値の信頼性が高まると同時に、
 * Demon のマジックバーチャルホスティングを機能させることが可能になります。
 * DNS 検索は十分に高速であるという前提に基づいています...
 * -- fanf 1998-10-03
 */

Original comment

/* There are two options regarding what the "name" of a server is.  The
 * "canonical" name as defined by ServerName and Port, or the "client's
 * name" as supplied by a possible Host: header or full URI.
 *
 * The DNS option to UseCanonicalName causes this routine to do a
 * reverse lookup on the local IP address of the connection and use
 * that for the ServerName. This makes its value more reliable while
 * at the same time allowing Demon's magic virtual hosting to work.
 * The assumption is that DNS lookups are sufficiently quick...
 * -- fanf 1998-10-03
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1182-1186

/*
 * URLで使用する目的で、リクエストから現在のサーバー名を取得します。
 * サーバー名がIPv6リテラルアドレスである場合、URL形式（例: "[fe80::1]"）で返されます。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Get the current server name from the request for the purposes
 * of using in a URL.  If the server name is an IPv6 literal
 * address, it will be returned in URL format (e.g., "[fe80::1]").
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1192

/* IPv6リテラルか？ */

Original comment

/* IPv6 literal? */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1232

/* USE_CANONICAL_PHYS_PORT_OFF または USE_CANONICAL_PHYS_PORT_UNSET */

Original comment

/* USE_CANONICAL_PHYS_PORT_OFF or USE_CANONICAL_PHYS_PORT_UNSET */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1220-1227

/* UseCanonicalName がオンの場合（および 1.3 より前のすべてのバージョンにおいて）、
             * Apache は ServerName ディレクティブで指定されたホスト名とポートを使用して、
             * サーバーの正規名を構築します。（ServerName ディレクティブでポートが指定されていない場合、
             * Apache はクライアントから提供されたポートがあればそれを使用し、
             * 最終的に使用されているプロトコルのデフォルトポートを使用します。）
             */

Original comment

/* With UseCanonicalName on (and in all versions prior to 1.3)
             * Apache will use the hostname and port specified in the
             * ServerName directive to construct a canonical name for the
             * server. (If no port was specified in the ServerName
             * directive, Apache uses the port supplied by the client if
             * any is supplied, and finally the default port for the protocol
             * used.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1253

//", host, uri, NULL);

Original comment

//", host, uri, NULL);

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1256

//%s:%u%s", ap_http_scheme(r), host, port, uri);

Original comment

Japanese translation

/*****************************************************************
 *
 * コマンド... このモジュールは NCSA httpd.conf のほぼすべての
 * コマンドを処理しますが、古い srm.conf の大部分は各モジュールに
 * 分割されています。
 */

Original comment

/*****************************************************************
 *
 * Commands... this module handles almost all of the NCSA httpd.conf
 * commands, but most of the old srm.conf is in the modules.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1331-1333

/* EXEC_ON_READ は NOT_IN_DIR_LOC_FILE でなければならない。そうでなければ、
             * （意図的に）以下の各テストでセグメンテーション違反が発生する...
             */

Original comment

/* EXEC_ON_READ must be NOT_IN_DIR_LOC_FILE, if not, it will
             * (deliberately) segfault below in the individual tests...
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1394

/* まあ、実際には何かをする必要がある */

Original comment

/* well, actually something to do */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1404

/* 次のエントリを準備 */

Original comment

/* prepare next entry */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1454

/* 結果を組み立てる */

Original comment

/* assemble result */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1477-1483

/*
 * 再起動時に Define/UnDefine の影響を元に戻せることを確認する。
 * この関数は、ap_server_config_defines が変更される前に、設定の読み込みごとに 1 回呼び出す必要がある。
 * これは設定の読み込み中に行われる可能性があり、その時点では (Define/UnDefine の EXEC_ON_READ により) pre_config フックが実行される前である。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Make sure we can revert the effects of Define/UnDefine when restarting.
 * This function must be called once per loading of the config, before
 * ap_server_config_defines is changed. This may be during reading of the
 * config, which is even before the pre_config hook is run (due to
 * EXEC_ON_READ for Define/UnDefine).
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1487-1489

/* unset_define によるエントリの削除方法に対して保護されないため、
     * apr_array_copy_hdr ではなく apr_array_copy を使用すること。
     */

Original comment

/* Use apr_array_copy instead of apr_array_copy_hdr because it does not
     * protect from the way unset_define removes entries.
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1722-1725

/* hack. msgの場合は " をプレフィックスとして付加する。これは、
             * http_protocol.c が msg と（ローカル）パスを区別するために
             * 依存している仕様であるため。
             */

Original comment

/* hack. Prefix a " if it is a msg; as that is what
             * http_protocol.c relies on to distinguish between
             * a msg and a (local) path.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:1770-1771

/* Includes が許可されている場合、Includes と
             * IncludesNOEXEC の両方を変更できます。 */

Original comment

             * IncludesNOEXEC may be changed. */

Japanese translation

/* AIの互換性維持。最悪 */

server/core.c:2047

/* Noneは他のオプションの後に続くことができないため、これのみが機能します。 */

Original comment

/* Only works since None may not follow any other option. */

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* Only works since All may not follow any other option. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2071

/* (d->opts_add & d->opts_remove) == 0 という不変条件を保証する */

Original comment

/* we ensure the invariant (d->opts_add & d->opts_remove) == 0 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2119-2123

/*
 * ファイルのETag値を生成する際に使用すべきデータに注意すること。
 * ITERATEとして実行できればより良いが、そうすると+/-の状態を適切に保持できなくなる。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Note what data should be used when forming file ETag values.
 * It would be nicer to do this as an ITERATE, but then we couldn't
 * remember the +/- state properly.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2151-2155

/*
             * 絶対設定が発生すると、それまでの相対設定はすべて
             * 上書きされます。最初にそのような設定が発生した時点で、
             * 継承された設定もすべて破棄されます。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * The occurrence of an absolute setting wipes
             * out any previous relative ones.  The first such
             * occurrence forgets any inherited ones, too.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2209-2212

/*
             * 'add' リストに含まれており、かつ 'subtract' リストに
             * 含まれていないことを確認してください。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * Make sure it's in the 'add' list and absent from the
             * 'subtract' list.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2221-2224

/*
             * 非相対値は、それまでに蓄積された
             * + または - の値をすべて消去します。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * Non-relative values wipe out any + or - values
             * accumulated so far.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2232-2234

/*
     * 何らかの設定を行うと、「None」および「Unset」ビットがクリアされます。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Any setting at all will clear the 'None' and 'Unset' bits.
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2350-2352

/*
 * 欠落した '>' の構文エラーを報告します。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Report a missing-'>' syntax error.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2359-2361

/*
 * '<Foo >' 構文エラーで引数が不足していることを報告します。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Report a missing args in '<Foo >' syntax error.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2393

/* ビルトインまたはモジュールに登録されたメソッド番号を確認する */

Original comment

/* check for builtin or module registered method number */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2400-2402

/* メソッドはまだ登録されていませんが、リソースの制限は
             * メソッドの処理前に必ずチェックされるため、ここで登録します。
             */

Original comment

/* method has not been registered yet, but resource restriction
             * is always checked before method handling, so register it.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2404

/* .htaccessでは、新しいメソッドをグローバルに登録することはできません。 */

Original comment

/* In .htaccess, we can't globally register new methods. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2416-2418

/* 1つの関数で2つの機能を処理する。
     * tog == NULL の場合は <Limit>、それ以外は <LimitExcept>
     */

Original comment

/* Killing two features with one function,
     * if (tog == NULL) <Limit>, else <LimitExcept>
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2440-2444

/* XXX: 誤り - 別の方法で行う必要がある（少なくとも OS2/Netware も同様の問題を抱えている!!!）
 * これを <DirectoryMatch> および <FilesMatch> で使用し、
 * 大文字と小文字を区別しない正規表現の一致によってユーザーが不利益を被らないようにする
 */

Original comment

/* XXX: Bogus - need to do this differently (at least OS2/Netware suffer
 * the same problem!!!
 * We use this in <DirectoryMatch> and <FilesMatch>, to ensure that
 * people don't get bitten by wrong-cased regex matches
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2750

/* 設定を初期化して取得する */

Original comment

/* initialize our config and fetch it */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2532-2535

/* これを明示的にする - "/" ルートは0個の要素を持つ。つまり、
     * 常にマージされ、常に最初にソートおよびマージされる。
     * その他はすべて、スラッシュの数によってソートおよびテストされる。
     */

Original comment

/* Make this explicit - the "/" root has 0 elements, that is, we
     * will always merge it, and it will always sort and merge first.
     * All others are sorted and tested by the number of slashes.
     */

Japanese translation

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2740-2743

/*
     * 他のディレクティブがconfigセクション内にあることを認識できるように、
     * ダミー値を設定します。

     * a config section.

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2804

/* 事前リンクされたものを検索 */

Original comment

/* search prelinked stuff */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2816

/* 動的な要素を検索 */

Original comment

/* search dynamic stuff */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2822-2837

/*
             * ap_find_loaded_module_symbol を呼び出すことが問題となるフェーズが2つあります。
             *
             * 設定の読み込み中、ap_server_conf は無効ですが、EXEC_ON_READ ディレクティブの
             * cmd->server から渡された場合、s はメインサーバー設定を指します。
             *
             * 設定の解析中、s がバーチャルホストであると、mod_so のバーチャルホスト用サーバー設定は
             * 後で初期化されるため、ap_find_loaded_module_symbol に渡すとセグメンテーション違反を
             * 引き起こす可能性があります。しかし、この時点では ap_server_conf は既に設定されています。
             *
             * そのため、s がバーチャルホストでない場合は s を使用し、s がバーチャルホストである場合は
             * ap_server_conf を使用します。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * There are two phases where calling ap_find_loaded_module_symbol
             * is problematic:
             *
             * During reading of the config, ap_server_conf is invalid but s
             * points to the main server config, if passed from cmd->server
             * of an EXEC_ON_READ directive.
             *
             * During config parsing, s may be a virtual host that would cause
             * a segfault in mod_so if passed to ap_find_loaded_module_symbol,
             * because mod_so's server config for vhosts is initialized later.
             * But ap_server_conf is already set at this time.
             *
             * Therefore we use s if it is not virtual and ap_server_conf if
             * s is virtual.
             */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2845

/* start_cond_section によって使用されるコールバック関数の型。 */

Original comment

/* Callback function type used by start_cond_section. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2848-2850

/* <IfXXXXX>形式の条件セクションの実装。条件をテストするためのコールバックはcmd->infoに格納されている必要があり、関数型test_cond_section_fnと一致していること。 */

Original comment

/* Implementation of <IfXXXXX>-style conditional sections.  Callback
 * to test condition must be in cmd->info, matching function type
 * test_cond_section_fn. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2892

/* start_cond_section の <IfModule> テストを実装するためのコールバック。 */

Original comment

/* Callback to implement <IfModule> test for start_cond_section. */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2913-2918

/*
     * 少なくともWindowsでは、テスト対象のパスが無効な場合（例：接続されていないUSBメモリ上のパスなど）、'ap_server_root_relative()' は NULL を返します。そのような場合は、ファイルが存在しないものと見なし、そのセクションをスキップするようにします。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * At least on Windows, if the path we are testing is not valid (for example
     * a path on a USB key that is not plugged), 'ap_server_root_relative()' will
     * return NULL. In such a case, consider that the file is not there and that
     * the section should be skipped.
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2935

/* httpd.conf コマンド... <VirtualHost> の処理から開始 */

Original comment

/* httpd.conf commands... beginning with the <VirtualHost> business */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:2960-2964

/* FIXME: ここにはまだ実装されていない機能があります。現在、単一の <VirtualHost> に複数のアドレスや名前を指定できるようになったため、共通の定義をグループ化し、個別の差異を持つ「サブホスト」を定義できるようにしたいと考えるかもしれません。しかし個人的には、マクロプリプロセッサで処理する方が好みです。 -djg */

Original comment

/* FIXME: There's another feature waiting to happen here -- since you
        can now put multiple addresses/names on a single <VirtualHost>
        you might want to use it to group common definitions and then
        define other "subhosts" with their individual differences.  But
        personally I'd rather just do it with a macro preprocessor. -djg */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3120

/* これはかなり汎用的なものです... */

Original comment

/* This one's pretty generic... */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3134-3140

/*
 * ServerName ディレクティブは、[scheme://]fully-qualified-domain-name[:port] 形式の引数を1つ取ります。例:
 * ServerName www.example.com
 * ServerName www.example.com:80
 * ServerName https://www.example.com:443
 */

Original comment

/*
 * The ServerName directive takes one argument with format
 * [scheme://]fully-qualified-domain-name[:port], for instance
 * ServerName www.example.com
 * ServerName www.example.com:80
 * ServerName https://www.example.com:443
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3390-3393

/* 注: ap_include_sentinel は DUMP_INCLUDES 中の ap_process_resource_config() でも使用されています。
     * 他方を更新せずに型を変更したり削除したりしないでください。
     */

Original comment

/* NOTE: ap_include_sentinel is also used by ap_process_resource_config()
     * during DUMP_INCLUDES; don't change its type or remove it without updating
     * the other.
     */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3421-3424

/* 注: ap_include_lineno は DUMP_INCLUDES 中に ap_process_resource_config() によって使用されます。
         * 型を変更したり削除したりする場合は、必ずもう一方も更新してください。
         */

Original comment

/* NOTE: ap_include_lineno is used by ap_process_resource_config()
         * during DUMP_INCLUDES; don't change its type or remove it without
         * updating the other.
         */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3446

/* recursion level done */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3550-3556

/*
 * ServerレスポンスヘッダーフィールドにサーバーのOSプラットフォームを含めるリクエストを処理します（ServerTokensディレクティブ）。残念ながら、設定をhttp_mainに伝達して文字列内の適切な場所に情報を挿入できるようにするため、新しいグローバル変数が必要になります。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Handle a request to include the server's OS platform in the Server
 * response header field (the ServerTokens directive).  Unfortunately
 * this requires a new global in order to communicate the setting back to
 * http_main so it can insert the information in the right place in the
 * string.
 */

Japanese translation

server/core.c:3603-3607

/*
         * バージョン文字列がnullの場合、プール破棄時にポインタをリセットするクリーンアップを登録する。
         * また、NULLの場合は元のSERVER_BASEVERSION文字列を追加していることも判明している。

Original comment

/*
         * If the version string is null, register our cleanup to reset the
         * pointer on pool destruction. We also know that, if NULL,
         * we are adding the original SERVER_BASEVERSION string.
         */

 * このルーチンは、実際のサーバーのベース識別子をバナー文字列に追加し、

 */

 * and then locks out changes until the next reconfig.
 */

 * XXX これらはすべてのプラットフォーム向けではなく、一部のUnix MPMであっても特定のディレクティブを必要としない場合があります。
 */

 * pay attention to.

 * some directives.
 */

 */

     */

Original comment
